races.race_name: Rod Gaskin Garden Machinery Handicap

runners.name: Sturlasson
runners.spotlight: Did well for new yard last autumn, winning three times (5f, AW/good to soft), and he has run some solid races in defeat in 2026; this will be just his third run at 6f and others bring more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Had a good autumn in 2025; running well in defeat this year; 6f the query

runners.name: Seven Questions
runners.spotlight: Ended a two-year barren spell when getting up late in a 5f handicap here 16 days; 3lb higher mark to deal with but he has a Group 3 win on his record and conditions won't faze him; respected.
runners.comment: Arrived on scene late when winning over 5f here last month; good chance despite 3lb rise

runners.name: Sudden Flight
runners.spotlight: Better on AW than turf but handicapped accordingly and he ran his best race on grass when third of seven at Windsor 13 days ago (good to firm); down the field in two runs on soft but his Ayr Silver Cup effort wasn't too bad; new cheekpieces could give him a lift.
runners.comment: Best turf run when 3rd at Windsor 13 days ago; more needed for the application of headgear

runners.name: Brosay
runners.spotlight: Won 6f handicaps at Windsor and Ascot last summer; dropped down the weights in defeat this season but there have been glimmers of promise and ground softer than good will be of benefit; ran no sort of race at Carlisle eight days ago (in cheekpieces) but his usual blinkers are back on today; Oisin Murphy booked; interesting.
runners.comment: Flopped last week but usual blinkers back on & he's on a dangerous mark; revival possible

runners.name: Fleetwater
runners.spotlight: Both wins at Windsor but she is effective elsewhere; runner-up in two of her three starts this year but she's back from nine weeks off and could prove vulnerable.
runners.comment: Both wins at Windsor but effective elsewhere; absent nine weeks; could prove vulnerable
runners.owner: Ms H N Pinniger And Partner

runners.name: Jungle Drums
runners.spotlight: Generally struggled for this yard but the notable exception came when dead-heating at Chelmsford last October (6f, AW); beaten 19l at Doncaster on his return ten weeks ago; not a solid option.
runners.comment: Dead-heated on AW last October but that a rare beacon of light since joining this yard
runners.owner: Jim And Claire Ltd And Jon & Kay Collins

runners.name: Solar Aclaim
runners.spotlight: Sprinter who is happiest on slow ground and gave some encouragement returned to 6f on soft at Epsom yesterday, where he finished mid-division having been squeezed out at the start; 4lb lower than for his last win.
runners.comment: 6f and slow ground are major positives; some encouragement at Epsom yesterday

runners.name: Toyotomi
runners.spotlight: Good third off this mark at Thirsk (6f, good) last week, doing best of those drawn in single figures and that despite meeting some trouble in running; has plenty of form on soft in France; one to take seriously.
runners.comment: Good third off this mark at Thirsk last week, meeting some trouble; slower ground a plus

races.race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: My A'Ali Baba
runners.spotlight: Looked an unlikely winner for the first 4.5f of his Chepstow debut (6f, good to soft) but the penny dropped in the closing stages and he ultimately ran out a decisive winner; the bare form is nothing special and he will need more to defy a penalty but he is open to improvement.
runners.comment: Green and going nowhere 2f out but ran on well to score at Chepstow last month; chance

runners.name: Bulletsnap
runners.spotlight: 85,000euros foal; second foal; by Minzaal; dam French Listed-placed 7.5f-8.5f winner (including 2yo/AW; RPR 93), sister to useful French 1m-1m4f winner Anobe; betting should be revealing for a stable in fine form.
runners.comment: 85,000euros foal; dam a Listed-placed winner; powerful yard going well; check betting

runners.name: The Harv
runners.spotlight: 65,000euros yearling, £260,000 breeze-up 2yo; second foal; half-brother to Listed-placed 5f 2yo winner Awraad (RPR 78); dam 5f/6f winner (including 2yo/AW; 88); Irish trainer saddled a winning 2yo newcomer on Wednesday; Oisin Murphy booked; of obvious interest.
runners.comment: £260,000 breeze-up 2yo; half-brother to Listed-placed 2yo winner Awraad; newcomer of note

runners.name: Asgar
runners.spotlight: Smart pedigree but in need of the experience when down the field in a 6f maiden at Newbury last month (good ground; 12-1); this looks less demanding and he is open to improvement.
runners.comment: No threat on Newbury debut last month but bred to do much better at some stage

runners.name: Oakford
runners.spotlight: In need of the experience when fifth of six at Salisbury (5f, good) 24 days ago, finishing a neck in front of the reopposing Mobadir; open to improvement over this extra furlong.
runners.comment: Closely matched with Mobadir on Salisbury debut form (5f, good); open to improvement

runners.name: Mobadir
runners.spotlight: Left trailing over 1f out on his Salisbury debut (5f, good) 24 days ago, dropping out to finish last of the six runners (Oakford a neck and one position ahead in fifth); can do better and 6f should suit.
runners.comment: Left behind over 1f out on last month's Salisbury debut (5f, good); 6f should suit better

races.race_name: Goodwood Horseracing Club Membership Selling Stakes

runners.name: Leucothea
runners.spotlight: Beaten favourite in maidens at Bath (5.7f, firm) and Wolverhampton (6f, AW) but she hasn't run badly on either occasion; has the speed to cope with 5f and Oisin Murphy booked now she drops into selling company.
runners.comment: Beaten favourite in two maidens over further; respected dropped into a seller

runners.name: Undercover Affair
runners.spotlight: Promise in all four starts, keeping on for second in a 6f seller at Chester eight days ago (passed the reopposing Fortunate, who was better drawn, close home); untried on slower than good; leading form claims.
runners.comment: Shown winning potential in all 4 starts; closely matched with Fortunate on latest; chance

runners.name: Cavan Lady
runners.spotlight: 25,000gns yearling; fourth foal; half-sister to winners Nibras Angel (7f; RPR 96) and Doha Bu Thaila (Qatari 6.5f-10.5f); dam 7f/1m winner (78), sister to 5f-7f winner Munfarrid, half-sister to Listed-placed 6.5f 2yo/1m winner Morning Post; useful pedigree for one starting out in selling company; betting to guide.
runners.comment: 25,000gns half-sister to two winners; realistic assignment on debut; betting to guide

runners.name: Fortunate
runners.spotlight: Poor form on her first three starts but she showed much more when fourth of 12 in a Chester seller (6f, good) eight days ago, prominent throughout and only losing second in the closing stages; now has something to build on and can go well back at 5f.
runners.comment: Big step forward when 4th at Chester eight days ago; return to 5f should suit; contender

runners.name: Beach Ahead
runners.spotlight: Never a threat but it was still a promising start to his career when fourth of 12 in a 6f maiden at Windsor two weeks ago (good to firm); quickly drops into selling company and he is open to improvement but others have achieved more.
runners.comment: Debut 4th at Windsor was encouraging; down to 5f and dropped into a seller; can do better

races.race_name: Weatherbys/British EBF Agnes Keyser Fillies' Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Sacred Ground
runners.spotlight: Probably acts on good to soft; won debut and runner-up in Listed events on all three starts since; this season's were over 1m2f at Newmarket (good to firm) and Newbury (good) and the latest form makes her one of the top two in this field on ratings; should be bang there again.
runners.comment: Won debut and runner-up in Listed events on all three starts since, twice over 1m2f as 3yo

runners.name: Rose Ghaiyyath
runners.spotlight: Has Group 1 entries in Coronation Stakes (1m) and Irish Oaks (1m4f); won newcomers' event at Deauville (7f, soft) last August and a rallying fourth in the Group 2 May Hill at Doncaster (1m, good to soft) on her only other race as 2yo; never-dangerous tenth of 19 at 50-1 in the 1,000 Guineas at Newmarket (1m, good to firm) on reappearance but she stuck to her task, beaten about 6l, and should stay 1m2f; a leading contender, including on form.
runners.comment: No ground worries; stuck to her task, beaten about 6l, when tenth at 50-1 in 1,000 Guineas

runners.name: Botagoz
runners.spotlight: Won novice events on first two starts, latterly at Nottingham (1m, good) in April; good third of seven in Listed race at York (1m, good) last month, with major late gains from out the back to suggest this longer trip will prompt further improvement; it is needed but she is one to consider.
runners.comment: Third of seven in Listed race at York (1m, good) with major late gains from out the back

runners.name: Plaid
runners.spotlight: Has improved a chunk with each of her four runs, last time winning off a handicap mark of 75 at Hamilton (good; unraced on softer) on the step up from 1m to 1m1f; as that win was easily by 8l, she has put herself into serious each-way calculations at this much higher level.
runners.comment: 1m1f handicap win was easily by 8l, so she has put herself into serious e-w calculations

runners.name: Lady Dora Mae
runners.spotlight: Not proven on softer than good; ridden by Jack Callan (who can't claim in this race) on last four outings; won three of her eight races, including over 1m at Windsor on penultimate start, and best form when upped to 1m2f for second at Nottingham on latest; that handicap form, however, leaves her with major work to do.
runners.comment: Best form when up to 1m2f latest but that handicap form leaves her with major work to do

runners.name: Light Of Paris
runners.spotlight: 500,000gns yearling by Sea The Stars; made a low-key debut on Kempton AW (1m3f, AW) in April but always prominent when winning a steadily run, seven-runner maiden at Ascot (1m2f, good) one month ago in tidy fashion; should improve again but this demands much better even though the second and fifth won maidens next time.
runners.comment: Won steadily run, seven-runner maiden at Ascot (1m2f, good) one month ago in tidy fashion

runners.name: Della Pace
runners.spotlight: 2yo maiden winner whose best form (from six races) came when fourth of eight in a C&D Listed race (good) two weeks ago; slowly away at 40-1 that day and made late progress, so still looks capable of a bit better; that could still mean minor honours, though.
runners.comment: 40-1 fourth of eight in C&D Listed race two weeks ago, slowly away before late progress

runners.name: Venetia
runners.spotlight: Made all at Ascot (1m2f, good to firm) on handicap/seasonal debut and set steady pace when second here (1m4f, good; unraced on softer) 16 days ago; steady improver but needs a big step forward in today's first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Improver (1m2f/1m4f handicaps this term) but needs much more in today's first headgear

runners.name: Ourbren
runners.spotlight: Pretty useful form in Listed races on last two starts as 2yo and she duly won a Bath maiden this April; never-dangerous sixth of ten in Group 2 German 1,000 Guineas (1m, soft) latest is her best form and this new trip is not out of the question, but others have accomplished more and/or shown more potential.
runners.comment: Never-dangerous sixth of ten in Group 2 German 1,000 Guineas (1m, soft) is her best form

races.race_name: Billy Vigar Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Russian Rumour
runners.spotlight: 10-58 overall for this 9yo, with none of those races at this track; can make the running; won at Ffos Las (2m, heavy) last September (by 12l) and Nottingham (1m6f, good) this May; not far away when third of four at Lingfield 12 days ago in hat-trick bid and has an each-way chance.
runners.comment: Not far away when third of four at Lingfield 12 days ago in her hat-trick bid

runners.name: Maxident
runners.spotlight: Effective here; goes well on testing ground, as he showed again when winning at Ffos Las (1m6f; stays 2m+) in September on latest start; 3lb rise today is far less concerning than a move away from soft or heavy ground; big player if conditions are in his favour.
runners.comment: Won in September last time; a move away from soft or heavy ground would be concerning

runners.name: Hermetic
runners.spotlight: Dual soft-ground winner (1m4f/2m) in France last autumn, from just four races there; picked up for 105,000euros in November and put up a bold show when fourth of 17 at York (1m4f, good) last month; stuck to his task at 20-1 that day and looks a strong candidate if that run has brought him on.
runners.comment: Strong candidate if his fourth of 17 at York (1m4f, good) last month has brought him on

runners.name: In The Breeze
runners.spotlight: Had a 1m4f win for Mick Appleby last September and another for Henrietta Knight at Thirsk this April; however, no show in a more competitive race on latest start, has never won over further than 1m4f and, although probably stays 1m6f, his sole attempt at 2m (final start last term) was not persuasive.
runners.comment: Probably stays 1m6f but his sole attempt at 2m (final start last term) was not persuasive

runners.name: Youthful King
runners.spotlight: 6lb lower than when winning at Sandown (1m6f, good to firm) last June under Jack Dace, who frequently rides him; has not beaten many in his four starts this season, however; has one piece of form at this track (2022) but something to prove on softer than good.
runners.comment: Won off 6lb higher last June; has not beaten many in his four starts this season, however

runners.name: Across Earth
runners.spotlight: Two hurdle wins late last year and he's held his form pretty well in that discipline, last seen six weeks ago; his last Flat race was in 2024 for his former yard and peak form that year (1m6f win) suggests this is a realistic mark.
runners.comment: Fit from hurdling; peak 2024 Flat form (1m6f win) suggests this is a realistic mark

runners.name: Call My Bluff
runners.spotlight: Won here in 2020 but last in this race last year; back to life at Newbury (2m, good; fine on soft) this April, making all in a six-runner race, but a sluggish start immediately put him on the back foot in the Chester Plate (10-1) one month ago and he ended up last of 17; well treated on old form but perhaps needs things to go his own way these days.
runners.comment: First and last in his last two outings; perhaps needs things to go his own way these days

runners.name: Alfred Boucher
runners.spotlight: Pipped in the 2022 Ebor; he's now a 10yo and very lightly raced in recent seasons but had sunk way down the weights before winning pretty smoothly at Salisbury (1m6f) last month on third start for this yard; bit to prove these days on softer than good; also back up 4lb but stays 2m and still thrown in judged on his old form.
runners.comment: Down the weights when winning pretty smoothly at Salisbury (1m6f) last month; stays 2m

races.race_name: Tapster Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Hamish
runners.spotlight: 10yo who's won a hatful of Group 3s at 1m4f-1m6f in his long and distinguished career, including at Newbury in October when last seen; has been kept to ground softer than good for a long time; kicked off that 2025 campaign with a win in this race and this star has good chance to repeat the dose.
runners.comment: Star veteran who was 3-4 last year, kicking off with a win in this race; good chance again

runners.name: Tenability
runners.spotlight: Won a novice and three Ascot 1m4f handicaps on varied ground last June-September; pipped a below-form Hamish for second in Ascot Group 3 on final outing last year and resumed his progress when he reappeared with a close third in Newbury Group 3 (1m4f, good) in April; that puts him into very serious contention today.
runners.comment: Close third in Newbury Group 3 (1m4f, good) in April puts him into very serious contention

runners.name: Arabian Crown
runners.spotlight: 2024 Sandown Classic Trial winner (good to soft) who missed the rest of that season; underwhelming on first four starts last year and ran particularly poorly at Glorious Goodwood (sole visit here) but he ended that campaign by winning narrowly in a four-runner Listed race at Windsor in August; needs to build on that but should stay 1m4f.
runners.comment: Finally back on the right track with his narrow Listed win at Windsor in August last time

runners.name: Involvement
runners.spotlight: Missed 2025; started this season at Meydan and Longchamp but returned to British soil with career-best form 16 days ago when a close fourth of nine in 1m2f Listed race at this track; more to prove on softer than good and this is also his first appearance beyond 1m2f, but that latest run gave some encouragement; either way he needs to find a bit extra.
runners.comment: Best form 16 days ago when a close fourth in 1m2f Listed race here; first go beyond that

runners.name: Glen Buck
runners.spotlight: 7yo who has had only six races, winning four; not discredited in April 2025 (1m2f; best form at 1m4f) when last seen but this graduation from handicaps seems a stiff task and it's also his turf debut.
runners.comment: 7yo who has had only six races, winning four; turf debut and stiff task out of handicaps

runners.name: Midak
runners.spotlight: Won his first three races in France last year and started just 9-1 when brought over for the Derby but faded badly into tenth; ran respectably over 1m1f (soft) in October on his only subsequent start for Francis Graffard but an also-ran in the UAE this year over 1m1f (Group 1) and 1m2f (Listed) on both starts since 300,000euros sale in November; plenty to prove.
runners.comment: 9-1 for 2025 Derby but faded badly into tenth; plenty to prove on evidence for new yard

races.race_name: Elston Support Your Local Air Ambulance Handicap

runners.name: Dannick
runners.spotlight: 14-race maiden; no impact in his two runs this year (following a wind operation) and the addition of cheekpieces isn't enough to tempt.
runners.comment: 0-14 and he needs to leave this year's two runs well behind him

runners.name: Blue Prince
runners.spotlight: Has some strong C&D form; the second half of 2025 was disappointing but he dropped in the weights and his reappearance third at Kempton was a welcome return to form; big player.
runners.comment: Has strong C&D form; returned to action with his third at Kempton; big player

runners.name: Serenity Dream
runners.spotlight: Win here last August (6f) came off 2lb lower; several good runs this year, including in a big field at Ascot four weeks ago, but others look stronger for win purposes.
runners.comment: Course winner; several good runs this year but others look stronger for win purposes

runners.name: Diamondonthehill
runners.spotlight: Capable of useful form but not easy to win with these days, getting little respite from the handicapper; another solid effort when second at Musselburgh 17 days ago (7f, good) but others appeal more for win purposes.
runners.comment: Infrequent winner these days but reliable and latest second was a solid effort; e-w shout

runners.name: Dr Strangelove
runners.spotlight: His two wins have come over 6f but he is fully effective over this extra furlong; back to his last winning mark and he will be a danger to all if the ground is suitable (well beaten in one run on slower than good).
runners.comment: On a winning mark and returning to 7f no problem; well held in one run on slower than good

runners.name: Cherry Cobbler
runners.spotlight: Capitalised on his reduced mark when running away with a 7f handicap at Newmarket three weeks ago (good ground); hit with an 8lb rise but he has had few chances at the trip; untested on slower than good.
runners.comment: Ready 7f win at Newmarket three weeks ago; up 8lb and ground softer than good is a query

runners.name: Tronido
runners.spotlight: Capitalised on the drop to Class 6 company when running away with a Lingfield handicap (7f, good) 19 days ago; the 7lb rise against stiffer opposition should find her out.
runners.comment: Easy win in Class 6 company last month; vulnerable up 7lb against stronger opposition

runners.name: Rare Change
runners.spotlight: Had bad luck in running over C&D during an otherwise successful spell around this time last year; inconsistent since but has possibilities off current mark with cheekpieces given another spin.
runners.comment: Thrived in the first half of 2025; inconsistent since but good mark and headgear returns
runners.owner: Mrs J Woods And K Lawrence

runners.name: Wobwobwob
runners.spotlight: Losing run stretches back over two years and neither start this year suggests he is ready to exploit his lowly mark; the prospect of slow ground does pique interest though.
runners.comment: On good mark and the prospect of slow ground piques interest in him; still risky though

races.race_name: Lynn Lodge Stud Irish EBF Maiden

runners.name: Cleodolinda
runners.spotlight: Ten Sovereigns filly sent off at 8-1 on debut over C&D (yielding) and found just Matriarchal (who is closely matched with Hanney Boy) too good by 0.5l; dwelt at the start that day and made up lots of ground in the final furlong; should be more streetwise now and she's a leading contender.
runners.comment: Fine second on debut over C&D when a bit slowly away, wiser now, leading contender

runners.name: Hanney Boy
runners.spotlight: Promise both starts; stayed on well for a 5.5l second on debut at Cork (soft) and the third from that race won a maiden here since (Cleodolinda second); beaten favourite upped to 6f back at Cork (soft) when racing keenly; this stiff 5f could be ideal; leading claims.
runners.comment: Runner up both starts at Cork on soft over 5f and 6f, this stiff 5f should suit, claims

runners.name: Desert Alchemist
runners.spotlight: Ghaiyyath gelding made a pleasing debut when a 2.75l third over 6f at Cork last month (Hanney Boy second), not quite getting home; the drop back to 5f should suit and he's a player.
runners.comment: Pleasing debut over 6f at the Curragh, drop back to 5f sure to suit, big player

runners.name: Away And Gone
runners.spotlight: Sent off the 3-1 joint favourite on debut at Cork (5f, soft) but failed to run to market expectations; likely capable of better and interesting to see if her backers return, but can only be watched after that initial effort.
runners.comment: Beaten joint favourite on debut at Cork, likely capable of better but can only watch now

runners.name: Rejoinder
runners.spotlight: Space Blues colt; E92,000 foal, E82,000 2yo; closely related to 6f 2yo/1m Listed winner Thunder Beauty (RPR 109), half-brother to three winners inc Haku (prolific 1m4f AW; 101) and Quita (German 1m3f-1m6f); dam German 1m3f/1m4f winner; respected source and worth monitoring in the market.
runners.comment: Space Blues colt, closely related to 6f 2yo/1m Listed winner Thunder Beauty, check market

runners.name: Spirit Of Mann
runners.spotlight: Havana Grey colt; 160,000gns 2yo; dam 7f AW 2yo winner (RPR 76), half-sister to winners at 1m and 1m/8.7f, out of sister to 1m Group 1 winner Timepiece, half-sister to 1m2f 2yo Group 1 winner Passage Of Time; promising second in Naas barrier trial last month; very interesting debutant with Keane a notable booking.
runners.comment: 160,000gns Havana Grey Colt, dam 7f 2yo winner, second in barrier trial, interesting

runners.name: Arrowstock
runners.spotlight: Cotai Glory filly; E17,000 yearling; dam 6f/7f winner (inc 2yo; RPR 90), out of maiden half-sister to 6f Listed winner Inyordreams and Listed-placed 5f 2yo winner Fire Eyes; best watched on debut unless the market speaks.
runners.comment: Cotai Glory filly, dam 6f/7f 2yo winner, watch on debut unless market speaks

runners.name: Come On Noddy
runners.spotlight: Profitable colt; E4,000 yearling; half-brother to 7f 2yo/9.5f AW winner Easily Convinced (inc France; RPR 71); dam French 1m2f 2yo winner (77), half-sister to 1m2f 2yo winner The Olympian; yard had recent 2yo winner; check market.
runners.comment: Profitable colt, dam French 1m2f 2yo winner, worth a market check on debut

runners.name: Howlin Wolf
runners.spotlight: 40-1, ran green and beaten 21l over 6f here on debut three weeks ago; may need more time.
runners.comment: 40-1, ran green and beaten 21l on debut here last month, may need more time

runners.name: Queenofthewild
runners.spotlight: Sent off at 7-1 on debut at Cork but never landed a blow; 6.5l behind Hanney Boy that day and major improvement required to turn that form around.
runners.comment: Never landed a blow on debut at Cork, well held by Hanney Boy, others preferred

races.race_name: Navan Racing Festival Early Bird Tickets On Sale Now Handicap

runners.name: Namiid
runners.spotlight: Gained breakthrough win at the tenth attempt in a C&D maiden (yielding) on latest in first time cheekpieces; now sports blinkers for first run in handicap company and talented claimer takes off a handy 5lb; can't discount.
runners.comment: C&D maiden win latest, blinkers replace cheekpieces, h'cap debut, can't discount

runners.name: Kerdos
runners.spotlight: High-class form for Clive Cox included a Group 2 win at Haydock in 2024; hasn't won since and last three runs for this stable not encouraging; mark sliding but needs to rediscover best; tongue tie tried now.
runners.comment: Group 2 winner in 2024, no joy since, poor form this year, tongue tie tried now
runners.owner: Michael Scaife/Liam Ferguson/Simon Murphy/H S Khunkhuna

runners.name: Cuban Grey
runners.spotlight: Back to winning ways off 4lb lower at Dundalk on latest, making all to score by 0.5l; may find things tougher upped in grade off his revised rating (never won off a mark this high).
runners.comment: All the way win on AW latest, raised 4lb and moves up in grade, tough mark now

runners.name: The Highway Rat
runners.spotlight: Five-time AW winner; both turf wins have come over this C&D on yielding and soft ground; pleasing seasonal return over 6f at the Curragh last month, not far behind Bodhi Bear; eased 1lb, yard in fine form and leading claims.
runners.comment: Two-time C&D winner, fine run on seasonal return, eased 1lb, yard in form, claims

runners.name: Erosandpsyche
runners.spotlight: No win since 2022; hasn't looked like regaining the winning thread in three runs this term; behind The Highway Rat and Bodhi Bear at the Curragh (6f) latest; eased 2lb and the drop back to 5f is a plus, but the forecast rain is a concern.
runners.comment: No win since 2022, behind The Highway Rat latest, return to 5f a plus, rain a worry

runners.name: Stag Night
runners.spotlight: In fine form this season at the Curragh; third on seasonal reappearance (6f, heavy); 1.25l second behind Bodhi Bear on penultimate (5f, sft-hvy); won over 5f (soft) there on latest and the runner up has run well in Listed company since; 5lb rise fair and should be bang there again if the forecast rain materialises.
runners.comment: Form of Curragh win on latest boosted by the second, up 5lb, go well again if rain comes

runners.name: Bodhi Bear
runners.spotlight: Ground-versatile sort; won for the third time on seasonal return at the Curragh in March (5f, sft-hvy) off 8lb lower; not disgraced in Listed company at Naas; fine effort off revised mark back at the Curragh latest (6f, good) when ahead of The Highway Rat and Erosandpsyche; won his maiden here (6f) and should go well again.
runners.comment: Ground versatile, 6f course winner, had two of these behind latest, go well again

races.race_name: Book Your Hospitality Now For Navan Racing Festival Handicap

runners.name: Thurso
runners.spotlight: Three wins last year, at 6f and 7f; going was fast for last month's 5f Bath win on his final start for Richard Hughes; any ease might harm his chance on Irish debut.
runners.comment: Going was fast for last month's 5f Bath win, any ease might harm his chance on Irish debut

runners.name: Asdana
runners.spotlight: Uneven form since winning over this trip at Cork last August; respectable fourth at Bellewstown, down the field at this venue last time; others are more dependable.
runners.comment: Uneven form since winning over this trip at Cork last August, others are more dependable

runners.name: Plushy
runners.spotlight: Placed on first two starts in 5f maidens last year at Bellewstown (good) and Cork (good to firm); also placed twice at Dundalk in January; concerning that she was well below her best at Down Royal nine days ago.
runners.comment: Placed twice at Dundalk in January, well below her best at Down Royal nine days ago
runners.owner: Derek Murray/Poetry In Motion Syndicate/Patrick McGuinness

runners.name: Astute Power
runners.spotlight: Ordinary form other than a close third of nine in a C&D maiden at Navan on soft last October; in rear at the Curragh on return, plenty to prove a a result
runners.comment: Best run last year was over C&D on soft, in rear at the Curragh on return, plenty to prove
runners.owner: Exors of the Late Mrs S Power

runners.name: Viamonte
runners.spotlight: Placed in five maidens this year and also finished third in a C&D handicap last July; rider teamed up with his mother at Dundalk recently to provide her with a winning start to her training career.
runners.comment: Third in a C&D handicap last July, trainer/rider combination enjoyed a fine recent success

runners.name: Jazzit
runners.spotlight: Placed in four of his eight AW starts, ran well when fifth of 17 over 6f here on latest; could make his presence felt if the addition of a visor helps; cheekpieces discarded.
runners.comment: Placed in four of his eight AW starts, ran well when fifth of 17 over 6f here on latest

runners.name: Verified
runners.spotlight: 21-race maiden; has occasionally shown winning potential; placed three times last year for his former trainer; two moderate runs this season have dented confidence in his ability to score.
runners.comment: 21-race maiden, has occasionally shown winning potential; two moderate runs this season

runners.name: An Laochmor
runners.spotlight: Transferred his excellent AW form (4211) to turf when second over C&D six week ago; chance in this company is underlined by his victory over Jordan Breeze at Dundalk in April; shares a consistent recent profile with that rival and Alnofoor.
runners.comment: Transferred his excellent AW form to turf when second over C&D in April, leading contender

runners.name: Happy Henry
runners.spotlight: Consistent form in his last five races; probably best over 6f but he should be fine with this stiff 5f; definitely not without a chance following his third placings at this venue and Fairyhouse; has early-season form linked to Alnofoor; change of headgear now.
runners.comment: Consistent form in his last five races, probably best over 6f but not without a chance

runners.name: Lethimfly
runners.spotlight: 5f maiden winner at two; confined to two runs last season; made a pleasing return at Dundalk in March, disappointed there 12 days ago; first time with cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Ran only twice last year, made a pleasing return at Dundalk, disappointed there on latest

runners.name: Jordan Breeze
runners.spotlight: Has developed into a reliable sort this season; second to An Laochmor at Dundalk and also runner-up to Alnofoor at Bellewstown before a well-deserved Cork win; apprentice has a good record on him; has stronger credentials than most
runners.comment: Has developed into a reliable sort this season and has stronger credentials than most

runners.name: Mint Man
runners.spotlight: Won over 6f at Naas last summer and fair fourth over 5f at the same venue on his penultimate start; looked ill at ease on the bend at Listowel last Sunday; not a big price on his last three starts; cheekpieces on now instead of usual tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Not a big price on his last three starts, cheekpieces on now instead of usual tongue-tie

runners.name: Ballysax Lil' Mick
runners.spotlight: Won off 2lb lower at Tipperary last season (5f, good); failed to beat a rival when last seen at Dundalk in January; record when fresh suggests he might need this run.
runners.comment: Useful form in the second half of last season, absent since a poor AW run  in January

runners.name: Hugo's Girl
runners.spotlight: 15-race maiden, placed five times; last month's Curragh third was an encouraging display in a 20-runner field; will not need to improve much to win a handicap of this standard.
runners.comment: 15-race maiden, placed five times, last month's Curragh third of 20 was encouraging

runners.name: Jackie Jump Up
runners.spotlight: Best effort at two when placed over 6f at this track; needs to step up from a low-key handicap debut at Fairyhouse ten days ago.
runners.comment: Best effort at two when placed over 6f at this track, needs to step up from Fairyhouse run

runners.name: Harry's Hill
runners.spotlight: Nine-time winner is a 5f specialist; below his best in two starts last month and has been unplaced in eight races since a Tipperary win last July.
runners.comment: Nine-time winner is a 5f specialist, unplaced in eight races since Tipperary win last July

runners.name: Step Back In Time
runners.spotlight: All four of her wins have come over sprint distances at this track, most recently last October off a 4lb lower mark; should be better for last month's outing here; needs to reverse the form of that race with Happy Henry and Jazzit.
runners.comment: All four of her wins have come at this track, should be better for last month's outing

runners.name: Mickey The Steel
runners.spotlight: Has a decent record at this track, two wins and three seconds from nine visits; solid form this term except a moderate Curragh run over 7f; place chance now after last Sunday's third at Listowel; acts on good but best on soft or heavy.
runners.comment: Has a decent record at this track, solid form this term with one exception, place chance

runners.name: Sommelier
runners.spotlight: Dual AW winner; highlight of British form when second to Big Evs in a Listed race at York in May 2024; rated 99 at his peak. gained his first turf success off 72 when making all in a 20-runner contest over an extended 6f at the Curragh on Wednesday; has a fixed 7lb penalty; an additional 2lb claimed this time; this stiff 5f should be fine for him.
runners.comment: Has a fixed 7lb penalty for Wednesday's front-running Curragh win, stiff 5f should be fine

runners.name: Alnofoor
runners.spotlight: Returned from over four years off last year; has prospered in the second phase of his career, winning back-to-back at Navan (6f, heavy) and Bellewstown (5f, soft); continued in fine form when third in a 21-runner contest at the Curragh seven weeks ago; every chance if continuing in the same vein.
runners.comment: Has prospered in the second phase of his career, every chance if maintaining recent form

runners.name: Polar Bear
runners.spotlight: Slow starts have been an issue this season, including when third here in April, 1 1/4l behind Al Laochmor; poor effort when always near the back at Fairyhouse on Friday.
runners.comment: Slow starts have been an issue this season, poor effort at Fairyhouse on Friday

runners.name: Three Sixteen
runners.spotlight: Modest form last season, failed to make an impact in a claimer on stable/seasonal debut; not one of the more obvious candidates.
runners.comment: Failed to make an impact in a claimer on stable/seasonal debut, not an obvious candidate

runners.name: Caffu Zafeen
runners.spotlight: Placed over 6f/7f in Britain two seasons ago; off for almost a year; hard to fancy against that background.
runners.comment: Placed over 6f/7f in Britain two seasons ago, off for almost a year, hard to fancy

races.race_name: Navan Handicap

runners.name: Ocean's Breath
runners.spotlight: 6f Dundalk winner in April; showing when favourite for a premier handicap at Naas can be excused; got warm beforehand and then had to race alone; won a 7f rated race comfortably at Fairyhouse on Friday; drops back in trip and carries 7lb penalty now.
runners.comment: Scored in 7f rated race at Fairyhouse on Friday, drops in trip, carries 7lb penalty now

runners.name: Greek Flower
runners.spotlight: On a long losing streak (since October 2023) but shaped well on several occasions, including on seasonal debut at Leopardstown; less encouraging run at Gowran dents confidence in her prospects of adding to her tally.
runners.comment: Losing streak stretches back to 2023, often runs well but hard to be confident

runners.name: Nouvel Espoir
runners.spotlight: Has shown signs of a return to his best; backed up a second placing over 5f at Naas with a third over 5f at this venue, a showing that gives him an edge over Alfred Tennyson.
runners.comment: Has shown signs of a return to his best with placed efforts at Naas and this venue
runners.owner: Ms Yvonne Hennessy & Ms S O'Neill

runners.name: Collective Power
runners.spotlight: Six-time AW winner; was close up in third behind Clonmacash at Dundalk in April; second there on latest, reversing form with Clonmacash in the process; has the merit of being a C&D winner; definite chance for 10lb claimer who has made the frame in three of the five Irish races she has ridden in.
runners.comment: Best form at Dundalk, second there on latest, C&D winner, could go well with 10lb off
runners.owner: Exors of the Late Mrs S Power

runners.name: Gerrit's Gem
runners.spotlight: All three wins for Kieran Cotter came at Cork (6f-1m); a long way below best in two races this term.
runners.comment: Three wins at Cork for his previous trainer, a long way below best in two races this term

runners.name: Alfred Tennyson
runners.spotlight: Won 7f maiden for Aidan O'Brien; fair AW run in March when beaten less than a length in third over 6f; missed break when well-held fourth at this venue; needs to reverse the form of last Sunday's Listowel clash with I Bid You Ajou and Aurora Nova.
runners.comment: Needs to reverse the form of a recent Listowel clash with I Bid You Ajou and Aurora Nova

runners.name: Aurora Nova
runners.spotlight: Made the breakthrough over 6f two seasons ago; her three wins last term were all over 5f, two of them at Bellewstown, where she had a good comeback run in April; closely matched with Aurora Nova on last Sunday's running at Listowel.
runners.comment: Best form at Bellewstown, closely matched with Aurora Nova on recent running at Listowel

runners.name: I Bid You Ajou
runners.spotlight: Came good last summer with a quick-fire Fairyhouse hat-trick over 6f; touched off at Naas three starts ago; denied a clear run until too late in a premier handicap at the Curragh two weeks ago; generally dependable sort was one place in front of Aurora Nova at Listowel last Sunday.
runners.comment: In the form of his life last summer when he won three in a row, generally dependable sort

runners.name: Clonmacash
runners.spotlight: Beat I Bid You Ajou at Dundalk in April; his AW record (5-30, placed nine times) is much stronger than turf (1-22, placed three times); only limited appeal with those statistics in mind.
runners.comment: Beat I Bid You Ajou at Dundalk in April, AW record (5-30) much stronger than turf (1-22)

runners.name: Jon Riggens
runners.spotlight: Three-time Curragh winner over 5f/6f on varying ground; has also scored at this track; recent form is unconvincing; trainer may have a better chance with the apprentice-ridden Collective Power.
runners.comment: Three-time Curragh winner, has also scored at this track, recent form is unconvincing

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Hill Of Tara Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Wemightakedlongway
runners.spotlight: Last season's best performances came in Group 1s when runner-up in the Irish Oaks at the Curragh (1m4f, good) and very close fourth in the Prix de l'Opera at Longchamp (1m2f, very soft); flopped on reappearance at Cork; Haydock third was reassuring; however, best at 1m2f-1m4f.
runners.comment: Smart form last season, flopped on return, Haydock third was reassuring, best at 1m2f-1m4f

runners.name: Isaac Newton
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner at Goodwood; best juvenile form when staying on from off the pace to take fourth behind Pierre Bonnard in Group 1 Criterium de Saint-Cloud (1m2f, very soft) in October; shaped as if he needed the race when fourth of seven in a Listed event at Newmarket (1m1f, good) on return; eased when beaten in Derby Trial at Lingfield; should be competitive now.
runners.comment: Eased when beaten in Derby Trial at Lingfield, fourth to Pierre Bonnard in a 2yo Group 1

runners.name: Hotazhell
runners.spotlight: Ended his 2yo campaign with a Group 1 win, beating Delacroix in the William Hill Futurity; no wins last year, but managed a fine third in the Irish 2,000 Guineas and a fair fourth in the Irish Champion Stakes (1m2f); too keen when attempting 1m4f in the Arc; can win races at four and this appeals as a suitable starting point if his fitness holds up.
runners.comment: Group 1-winning juvenile; high-class form last year without winning, big chance if ready

runners.name: Geryon
runners.spotlight: Won Curragh maiden on debut (7f, good); later ran with credit in Group 2 juvenile events won by the Prix du Jockey Club principals, Constitution River and Hawk Mountain; yet to advance that profile in races at the Curragh and Leopardstown this season; premature to write off; hood added to tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Juvenile form ties in with the Prix du Jockey Club principals, yet to advance that profile

runners.name: Flushing Meadows
runners.spotlight: Placed in two small-field Group 3 races at two; question to answer on this year's form; held by Geryon on Leopardstown running; last of five behind Constitution River in the Dee Stakes; stablemate Isaac Newton is more appealing.
runners.comment: Placed in two small-field Group 3 races at two, questions to answer on this year's form
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Bubble Gum
runners.spotlight: Finished midfield in French and German Oaks in 2024; Listed winner at Cork for Paddy Twomey on Irish debut; failed to build on that; two moderate runs this season; needs big improvement in first-time blinkers.
runners.comment: Listed winner over 1m4f on Irish debut, failed to build on that, two moderate 2026 runs

runners.name: Lemsairbat
runners.spotlight: Poor strike rate of 1-19 but has run plenty of solid races in defeat; twice runner-up in Listed races this term, so not out of place here; chased home stablemate Sindria at Gowran last time, with Subsonic back in third.
runners.comment: Modest strike rate, twice runner-up in Listed races this term, not out of place here
runners.owner: H R D McCalmont & Hamish McCalmont & Andrew N C Bengough

runners.name: Summer Is Tomorrow
runners.spotlight: Looked well suited by 7f when winning twice at two; vulnerable at this level on the evidence of two runs this term.
runners.comment: Useful juvenile last year, vulnerable at this level on the evidence of two runs this term

runners.name: Norwalk Havoc
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner at 7f/1m, the last two at Listed level; Leopardstown specialist; considerably inferior to stablemate Hotazhell.
runners.comment: Leopardstown specialist, dual Listed winner, considerably inferior to stablemate Hotazhell

runners.name: Lady Lilac
runners.spotlight: Fair handicapper; 88-rated filly has found things too demanding on her first two attempts in this grade.
runners.comment: Fair handicapper, has found things too demanding on her first two attempts in this grade

runners.name: Subsonic
runners.spotlight: Second to the top-class Minnie Hauk on debut at Leopardstown in 2024 (1m, soft); off the mark on her fourth and final start of 2025 in a 1m2f Curragh maiden; achieved a clear career-best when third at Gowran a month ago, closely matched with Lemsairbat.
runners.comment: A clear career-best when third at Gowran a month ago, closely matched with Lemsairbat

runners.name: Hazdann
runners.spotlight: 7f maiden winner at two; not seen again after a promising fourth behind Henri Matissse on seasonal debut last year; worth a market check.
runners.comment: Not seen again after a promising seasonal debut last year, worth a market check
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

races.race_name: Darley Irish EBF Kooyonga Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies & Mares)

runners.name: Drop Dead Gorgeous
runners.spotlight: Looked smart when making a winning debut over 1m at Naas in March; a combination of inexperience and trouble in running proved her undoing in a 1m fillies' Group 3 at the Curragh early last month; has something to prove now after a lifeless display over 1m2f at the same venue last time.
runners.comment: Good 1m run at the Curragh before a lifeless display over 1m2f at the same venue
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: La Fogata
runners.spotlight: Useful form over 1m as a juvenile; beaten 5l in a six-runner Curragh Group 3, strong at the line when landing Punchestown maiden; heavy ground a possible excuse for a poor run on final start for Ger Lyons; finished in front of well-fancied stablemate Eretria when second in a Listed race over an extended 1m2f at Naas on first outing for this team.
runners.comment: Promising run in a Listed race over an extended 1m2f at Naas on first outing for this team

runners.name: Duckadilly
runners.spotlight: Second in a 1m Listed race at Killarney last August; aimed very high in the Matron Stakes and now has her sights lowered after contesting a 7f Group 3 event at Leopardstown four weeks ago; this extra furlong may prove closer to the optimum trip.
runners.comment: Has her sights lowered after contesting a 7f Group 3 event at Leopardstown, merits respect

runners.name: Quebec
runners.spotlight: Placed twice in Pattern races in Germany last year; has failed to make an impression in Pattern races won by the smart City Of Memphis; detached last of 12 in the Leopardstown race in which Sindria finished ninth.
runners.comment: Placed twice in Pattern races in Germany last year, no sign of best form in two 2026 runs

runners.name: Syzygy
runners.spotlight: Progressive handicapper on heavy ground late last season; best of her three runs this season when third in the Irish Lincoln; meets stablemate Duckadilly on 16lb worse than handicap terms; blinkered for the first time.
runners.comment: Progressive last autumn, meets stablemate Duckadilly on 16lb worse than handicap terms

runners.name: Sindria
runners.spotlight: Down the field in a Group 2 event at the Curragh a fortnight ago; had previously stamped herself as an improving filly with two Gowran wins, the second of which was in this grade, earning a 3lb penalty; 100-rated filly is joint top-rated on official ratings with two of the younger generation.
runners.comment: Down in class after a Group 2 bid, looked progressive before that, definite chance now

runners.name: Sanctijude
runners.spotlight: Consistent in maidens and handicaps before breakthrough win at Gowran on her tenth start; 81-rated filly faces a big task in this grade.
runners.comment: Maiden winner on her tenth start, 81-rated filly faces a big task in this grade

runners.name: Fingerpaint
runners.spotlight: Debut 7f winner at the Curragh early last season; fair third in Group 3 event at Tipperary last August, having been badly hampered turning in; probably unsuited by heavy ground when failing to make an impact at Leopardstown on her final start.
runners.comment: Good standard of form twice before struggling on heavy ground on final outing last year

runners.name: White Sand Beach
runners.spotlight: Placed in all four maiden attempts, including behind La Fogata last season and stablemate Drop Dead Gorgeous at Naas in March; last of 12 in a Group 3 event at the Curragh; hard to fancy at this level following an unplaced handicap run on latest.
runners.comment: Maiden showed plenty of promise at two, hard to fancy at this level on this season's form
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Yellowstone Lake
runners.spotlight: Went close on Leopardstown debut last July and then fourth of ten behind the brilliant Diamond Necklace over 7f at the Curragh; beat the useful Emmeleia in a maiden at Dundalk in September; looks almost sure to improve her official rating of 89, if she has trained on satisfactorily.
runners.comment: Promising at two, can improve her 89 official rating if she has trained on satisfactorily

runners.name: Fast Tara
runners.spotlight: Placed twice in handicaps this term; only one win from 26 starts; faces a huge task in this company.
runners.comment: Placed twice in handicaps this term, only one win from 26 starts, faces a huge task

races.race_name: Cusack Hotel Group Family Raceday July 11th Handicap (Div I)

runners.name: God The Highness
runners.spotlight: 0-10 on turf on the Flat; placed twice last season, over 1m6f here (soft) and over 13.5f (gd-yld) at Sligo; 2lb lower now and shaped okay in a Limerick maiden hurdle latest; cheekpieces on; could go well.
runners.comment: Placed twice last year inc' here, shaped ok in maiden hurdle latest, cheekpieces on

runners.name: Desert Friend
runners.spotlight: Veteran gained the ninth win of his career when bolting up at Sligo on penultimate (1m6f, yld-sft); raised 8lb and couldn't follow up at Listowel (2m, good); drop back in trip a plus and has run well here before; place shout.
runners.comment: Bolted up at Sligo on penultimate, no impact at Listowel latest, could rebound

runners.name: Proleek Prince
runners.spotlight: Two wins in 2024 but has been in the handicapper's grip since; not a bad run at Fairyhouse latest (1m2f, good) but still beaten 8l and didn't look to be crying out for this longer trip; down in grade but others appeal more.
runners.comment: No win since 2024, beaten 8l over 1m2f latest, this trip a query, others preferred

runners.name: Deluca Chop
runners.spotlight: Knocking on the door on the AW earlier this year; fine run on return to turf when a 1.75l second at Roscommon (Mapa behind); nudged up 1lb but should be thereabouts again; visor goes on.
runners.comment: Knocking on the door, fine second at Roscommon later, up 1lb, go well again; visored

runners.name: Sarmiento Power
runners.spotlight: 1m4f AW winner is 0-4 on turf; recent showings at Dundalk not encouraging and remains 7lb above sole win; others appeal more.
runners.comment: 1m4f AW winner, 0-4 on turf, remains 7lb above sole win, others appeal more

runners.name: Mapa
runners.spotlight: Maiden but has run well in defeat, including when a keeping-on 3l third of 16 on seasonal return at Roscommon on latest (1m4f, yld-sft); worth a go at this longer trip on that evidence; big player off the same mark.
runners.comment: Shaped like this trip might suit on seasonal return, same mark here, big player

runners.name: Kevin The Great
runners.spotlight: 0-9 for Jessica Harrington; showed promise on a couple of occasions off higher marks last year but two runs this year were poor; new yard now and tongue tie goes on; stamina to prove up in trip.
runners.comment: 0-9, poor form this year, new yard, tongue tie on, stamina a query for this trip

runners.name: Ethereal Star
runners.spotlight: Standout effort a 2.75l third at Killarney last July off 7lb higher but nowhere near that level in two runs this season; beaten 17.5l at Gowran on latest; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Third at Killarney off 7lb higher last July, poor so far this season, others preferred

runners.name: Maxwell Smart
runners.spotlight: Sole win came off this mark at Down Royal last September (1m5f, good); hasn't really fired this season and beaten 8.75l back at Down Royal latest (2m, good), but has gone well over this C&D before so not discounted.
runners.comment: On last winning mark, hasn't fired so far this season but has C&D form, can't discount
runners.owner: Mrs P McCourt/Whyte's Stamullen Synd'/D O'Leary/J McNally

runners.name: Idomything
runners.spotlight: Won off 2lb lower at Bellewstown last October (12.5f, soft); not a bad effort when midfield at Roscommon latest (1m4f, yld-sft); rain would boost chance.
runners.comment: Won off 2lb lower at Bellewstown last Oct', best form on soft ground, needs rain

runners.name: Bang Po
runners.spotlight: Won on heavy ground at Killarney (1m) in 2023 but no joy since; out of form this term and step up to 1m5f didn't elicit improvement at Leopardstown latest; others preferred.
runners.comment: Long losing run, no improvement for step up to 1m5f latest, others preferred

runners.name: El Regalo
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose maiden yet to trouble the judge in either code; soundly beaten on last two Flat starts and over hurdles on latest; drops in grade and blinkers return, but hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Dual-purpose maiden, last two Flat runs modest, down in grade but hard to fancy

runners.name: Spinning Web
runners.spotlight: Has lost way over hurdles and fences; won off 2lb lower at Dundalk (1m4f) in 2023 but 0-9 on turf on the Flat and others arrive in better form.
runners.comment: No Flat win since 2023, lost way over jumps recently, others arrive in better form

runners.name: Mr Mills
runners.spotlight: Two-time winner, including here over 1m2f (soft) but hasn't scored since 2023; fine effort when last sighted at Dundalk in November; off since and has a modest record when fresh but worth a market check.
runners.comment: No win since scoring over 1m2f here in 2023, good AW run when last seen, check the market

runners.name: Jazz Forever
runners.spotlight: AW winner is 0-11 on turf on the Flat; well below best over hurdles on last two starts and ran poorly when last seen on the level at Dundalk; hard to make a case for.
runners.comment: AW winner, turf maiden, in poor form over hurdles recently, hard to make a case for

runners.name: Lariat
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose; 2-38 on turf; no win since 2022 and was pulled up over hurdles at Sligo 26 days ago on return from a break; hard to make a case for.
runners.comment: On long losing run, pulled up over hurdles latest, hard to make a case for

races.race_name: Cusack Hotel Group Family Raceday July 11th Handicap (Div II)

runners.name: Jurality
runners.spotlight: Maiden running consistently well in defeat this year; closely matched with a couple of these rivals on Leopardstown third on penultimate and on latest Down Royal second (2m, good); nudged up 3lb but should continue to give a good account.
runners.comment: Maiden, in good form this year, nudged up 3lb for latest second, should run well again

runners.name: Eastern Wind
runners.spotlight: Knows how to win and shaped as though her turn was near when running on well for second at Leopardstown (ahead of Jurality and Felicity Smoak); big chance if she backs that up off the same mark.
runners.comment: Ran on for second at Leopardstown latest, big chance if backs that up off same mark

runners.name: Appian Way
runners.spotlight: Flat maiden has won two over hurdles; out of form in that sphere this year and while he has gone well here before, he's hard to fancy on recent showings; tame effort again over hurdles at Clonmel on Friday.
runners.comment: Flat maiden, out of form over hurdles this year, gone well here before but hard to fancy

runners.name: Nita
runners.spotlight: Three wins at 1m2f-1m3f in Germany but bar a Dundalk third last November, hasn't really threatened for this yard; off since December and returns with stable in decent fettle, so market check advised.
runners.comment: No joy yet for this yard, off since Dec', returns with stable in good fettle, check market

runners.name: Mephisto
runners.spotlight: Two solid runs since return from winter break; 4l third at Dundalk (1m4f), then 0.5l behind Jurality when third at Down Royal (Synchronize fourth); 2lb rise for that seems a touch harsh, but place claims again.
runners.comment: Two solid runs since return from break, 0.5l behind Jurality latest, up 2lb, place shout

runners.name: God Of Thunder
runners.spotlight: Won off 73 at Limerick last summer (12.5f, good); poor form since, though his penultimate run at Roscommon wasn't devoid of promise; not as good there on latest but now drops into 0-60 company and is on a dangerous mark.
runners.comment: Won off 13lb higher last summer, poor latest but drops in grade now, dangerous mark

runners.name: Just Another Eagle
runners.spotlight: 1m4f AW winner is 0-15 on turf; beaten a head over 1m6f here last October on soft off 2lb lower; 1m was inadequate on seasonal return at Gowran; should come on plenty for that and not discounted back up in trip.
runners.comment: Beaten a head off 2lb lower over 1m6f here last Oct', 1m too sharp latest, can't discount

runners.name: Talking Tough
runners.spotlight: Not the easiest to win with, as his 2-63 record on turf illustrates; never involved after a slow start in a QR race at Listowel latest; unproven at this trip.
runners.comment: Not easy to win with, very slowly away latest, unproven over this distance

runners.name: Felicity Smoak
runners.spotlight: Best effort yet on the Flat when a 3.75l sixth of 20 at Leopardstown (1m5f, good) on return from 139 days off, denied a clear run (1.5l behind Eastern Wind); sharper for that, same mark here and should be thereabouts with smoother passage.
runners.comment: Unlucky in running at Leopardstown on latest, same mark here, thereabouts with clear run
runners.owner: E Dillon & B Power & J J Whelan & K R Waplington

runners.name: Creative Dancer
runners.spotlight: Long-standing maiden showed a bit more when third at Sligo (10.5f, yld-sft) on latest when 5lb wrong; 3lb out of the handicap here and stamina to prove for this trip; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Better effort at Sligo latest, 3lb wrong, stamina to prove for this trip, others preferred

runners.name: Synchronize
runners.spotlight: Maiden produced one of his better efforts on latest when not far behind a couple of these rivals at Down Royal (2m, good); place shout if he backs that up, but overall profile suggests that's not guaranteed.
runners.comment: Not far behind a couple of these at Down Royal latest, needs to back that up now

runners.name: Mystic Rose
runners.spotlight: Maiden showed a glimmer of promise at Bellewstown in April but poor twice since; yet to prove she stays this trip and much more needed to get her head in front.
runners.comment: Maiden, failed to build on Bellewstown fourth, much more needed to get head in front

runners.name: Edge Of Darkness
runners.spotlight: 4-20 on turf but hasn't won since 2023; not a bad run over 2m at Listowel (2m, good) after missing the break latest; more needed back in trip, but positive rider booking.
runners.comment: No win since 2023, not a bad effort at Listowel latest, more needed, top rider booked

runners.name: Cahir Bay
runners.spotlight: Handicap debutante didn't show a whole lot in maidens at 9.5f-1m4f on varying ground; off since October and sports cheekpieces; best watched.
runners.comment: Handicap debutante, modest maiden form, off since Oct', cheekpieces on, watch

runners.name: Folly Beach
runners.spotlight: 1-23 on turf; beaten 7l in this last year and just 1lb lower now; off since poor run over hurdles at the Galway festival; may need this run.
runners.comment: Beaten 7l in this last year and just 1lb lower now, off since last July, may need this

races.race_name: Bermingham Cameras Novice Chase

runners.name: Supersundae
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdle winner placed in a Grade 1 before losing his form last year; blasted back to somewhere near his best when landing a Kilbeggan beginners' chase (2m7f, good to yielding) by 17l in April; 2m at Wexford was too sharp for him when 7.5l behind the winner in second, carrying a penalty; rider reduces penalty here and he's undoubtedly the one to beat.
runners.comment: Placed in Gr.1 over hurdles; easily won beginners' but 2m too sharp last time; big player

runners.name: Sky Lord
runners.spotlight: Rated 130 after five runs over fences without winning; beaten favourite at Cork on handicap chase debut (2m1f, good to yielding); 25l behind in a big-field Listed handicap chase here last time; needs to raise his game but could be best placed to take advantage if the top-rated disappoints.
runners.comment: 0-5 over fences and beaten 25l in a h'cap here last time; needs to raise his game.

runners.name: Ma Jacks Hill
runners.spotlight: Won his second point-to-point after unseating in his first; 1-5 over hurdles after landing a Gowran maiden in October (2m1f, soft) and is rated 120 over smaller obstacles; some encouragement in his chase debut at Limerick last time when an 18.5l third; others preferred.
runners.comment: Point winner was 1-5 over hurdles with 120 rating; okay chase debut but others preferred

runners.name: Jolie Jewel
runners.spotlight: Dual winning hurdler is rated 115 in that sphere; made a positive chasing debut at Ballinrobe last time when third and shaping as though the 2m1f offered by the race was inadequate; can improve but will need to do so radically in this company.
runners.comment: Made a positive chasing debut last time but a good chunk of improvement needed

runners.name: Uno Me I Like My T
runners.spotlight: All her form in maiden hurdles, points and a bumper says she's out of her depth here.
runners.comment: All her form in maiden hurdles, points and a bumper says she's out of her depth here

races.race_name: Jim Ryan Memorial Novice Chase

runners.name: Yoradreamer
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner over hurdles, rated 118; promise in good beginners' at Gowran and built on that to win four-runner affair over this trip at Wexford, making all; didn't get far in novice handicap at the festival here (hampered and unseated rider) for which he was 40s and Raglan Road was 18/1; wins have come on easy ground, but he handles good.
runners.comment: Made all in four-runner contest over this trip at Wexford; same tactics likely here

runners.name: Emily Love
runners.spotlight: Likes a sound surface and was prolific over hurdles (rated 129) last year, winning five times at up to 2m4f, including a fast-ground victory in America; was running a good race when coming down on chasing debut; benefitted from fall of odds-on favourite when winning over 2m at Tramore, not having much to spare over 112-rated rival.
runners.comment: Benefitted from fall of odds-on favourite when winning over 2m at Tramore

runners.name: Raglan Road
runners.spotlight: 131-rated hurdler was third to Wodhoodh in last year's Martin Pipe; won rated chase off 124 at Down Royal, pulled up in valuable novice handicap at the festival here last month; pulled up over hurdles on sole previous try over 3m, but that was on bad ground.
runners.comment: Won rated chase off 124 at Down Royal, pulled up in valuable novice handicap here since

races.race_name: K-Mech Mechanical Handicap Chase

runners.name: Vaureal
runners.spotlight: Beaten a head by The Gradual Slope at Fairyhouse in April (2m5f, yielding to soft); creditable effort around the cross-country course at the Punchestown festival last time (3m1f, good to yielding); not ruled out.
runners.comment: Beaten a head by The Gradual Slope on penultimate; creditable cross-country run latest

runners.name: He's Gorgeous
runners.spotlight: Didn't hang around over hurdles and won the second of his beginners' chase starts at Clonmel (2m2f, heavy) in December; fourth of nine in Grade 1 company next time at Leopardstown; kept plugging away when fine fifth of 22 in deep novice handicap at Punchestown festival in May (2m4f, yielding); never involved when favourite in a better Ballinrobe contest last time; blinkers go on, and he's of interest if they sharpen him.
runners.comment: Fifth of 22 in better contest here but PU last time; interesting if blinkers help

runners.name: Jalila Moriviere
runners.spotlight: One of three for trainer; trounced weak opposition on chase debut at Sligo in October on good to yielding; ran a nice race in a Grade 3 Thurles novice chase in March (2m6f, soft) and bounced back from a poor effort at Fairyhouse when a distant second of 20 in a 2m1f Listed contest last time; firmly in the mix.
runners.comment: Runner-up in 2m1f Listed contest here last time; firmly in the mix on that basis

runners.name: El Champo
runners.spotlight: Dual-winning chaser whose last win came at Listowel in September off 1lb lower (2m6f, good to yielding); looks up against it based on a failure to complete at Gowran over fences and Fairyhouse over hurdles before finishing tailed off here last time.
runners.comment: Dual-winning chaser is up against it based on three recent dismal runs

runners.name: Glenashling
runners.spotlight: One of three for trainer; maiden hurdle winner early last year; runner-up in six-runner Cork contest (2m1f, good to yielding) on handicap chase debut in April; another creditable effort back in a beginners' chase at Wexford (2m, good to yielding) last time, when well ahead of Mister Pink and Karoline Banbou.
runners.comment: Runner-up on handicap chase debut and creditable effort in beginners' last time

runners.name: Captain Conby
runners.spotlight: Had very useful form in 2023 and looked sharper for his Flat return after 851 days off the track when a 12l sixth of ten at Wexford (2m, good to yielding); on a nice mark if continuing to shake off his rustiness.
runners.comment: Good form in 2023 and improved over return from 851-day absence when sixth of ten latest

runners.name: Mister Pink
runners.spotlight: Bumper and maiden hurdle winner for Willie Mullins; three beginners' chase runs for new yard back from a break haven't been much to shout about; may do better now in a handicap with an opening mark 7lb below his hurdles one.
runners.comment: Bumper/maiden hurdle winner for Willie Mullins; beginners' chase runs have been middling

runners.name: Dutch Schultz
runners.spotlight: One of three for trainer; most recent of two chase wins came in a 2m1f Killarney handicap chase in August 2024; moderate efforts in the autumn and two outings back from a break haven't been much better.
runners.comment: Moderate efforts in the autumn and two runs back from a break haven't been much better

runners.name: Me Wee Bonnie Lass
runners.spotlight: One of three for trainer; three-time winning hurdler got off the mark over fences in some style when 10l winner of Listed mares' handicap at Fairyhouse (2m5f, yielding to soft); 8lb higher now, but big player if that win wasn't an aberration.
runners.comment: Off mark in some style over fences in a Listed race latest; up 8lb but player with repeat

runners.name: Karoline Banbou
runners.spotlight: One of three for trainer; beaten favourite in all four beginners' chase starts, the latest of which when 33l behind at Wexford after some jumps lacking in fluency; hard to trust and probably wants softer, but she's dangerous if putting it all together.
runners.comment: Beaten fav' in all four beginners'; hard to trust and wants softer but dangerous

runners.name: Ocastle Des Mottes
runners.spotlight: One of three for trainer; dual French hurdles winner is yet to score for this yard but has run well in defeat at times, including when runner-up at Punchestown on chasing debut (2m3f, soft); returned from over a year off at Cork (2m1f, yielding) when 23l fourth behind better-fancied stablemate; makes handicap chase debut now.
runners.comment: Dual French winner is yet to score for this yard; okay runs over fences; makes h'cap bow

runners.name: Listentillitellyea
runners.spotlight: Has won over hurdles but is 0-9 over fences; ground given as possible excuse for 45l defeat at Ballinrobe (2m1f, good) on return from 295-day break on latest; forecast rain a plus and has run respectably here before but others hold more pressing claims; sixth in 1.50 here on Saturday.
runners.comment: 0-9 over fences, good ground not ideal two runs ago, rain a plus but others appeal more

runners.name: The Gradual Slope
runners.spotlight: Recorded first win since 2024 when taking a Fairyhouse handicap over 2m5f last time despite jumping right late on; raised 5lb for that so will have to raise his game again, but has won off 4lb higher in the past.
runners.comment: Recorded first win for two years last time; up 5lb, so needs to raise his game again

races.race_name: Congratulations Nicole Lockhead Anderson INHSC Ladies Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Icare Desbois
runners.spotlight: Formerly useful for Wille Mullins and ran okay in Flat maidens in the autumn, but his recent hurdles form has been poor and mark on the slide.
runners.comment: Formerly useful for Wille Mullins; recent hurdles form has been poor and mark on the slide

runners.name: Fortunate Lighting
runners.spotlight: Won 2m5f Sligo handicap on good ground last summer and her reappearance fifth at Bellewstown wasn't a bad effort over a trip too short; tailed off on chase debut last month.
runners.comment: Won 2m5f Sligo handicap on good ground and reappearance fifth at Bellewstown wasn't bad

runners.name: Cut The Rope
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner on debut, yet to add to that; his fifth of 23 in 2m4f Leopardstown handicap over Christmas would give him a chance; well beaten on bad ground next time; had a spin on the Flat last month.
runners.comment: His fifth of 23 in 2m4f Leopardstown handicap over Christmas would give him a chance
runners.owner: Brian P Whelan & Thomas J Quinn

runners.name: Hidalgo Des Mottes
runners.spotlight: Plenty of placed efforts but is a long-standing maiden over hurdles; fair Flat run last month; only 11-2 at Clonmel on Friday, but was soundly beaten.
runners.comment: Plenty of placed efforts but long-standing maiden over hurdles; well beaten on Friday

runners.name: Adaliz
runners.spotlight: Two handicap wins on nice ground last summer, over a similar trip to this at Clonmel and then over nearly 3m at Tipperary; off 10 months.
runners.comment: Two handicap wins on nice ground last summer, over nearly 3m on second occasion; layoff

runners.name: Fairlander
runners.spotlight: Placed in a decent point and ran well on handicap debut when third of 19 at Down Royal (2m4f, yielding) in March; well held at the festival here, but should be competitive back down in grade
runners.comment: Ran well on handicap debut when third of 19 at Down Royal (2m4f, yielding)
runners.owner: David L'Estrange & Eric Newnham & William Esdaile

runners.name: That's About Right
runners.spotlight: Three hurdle wins but this is his first run in this discipline since 2024; couple of fair runs over fences this year and will like the ground.
runners.comment: Three hurdle wins but this is first run in this discipline since 2024; good ground to suit

runners.name: Lakewood Rose
runners.spotlight: Point winner has been fourth in first two handicaps and only beaten around 3l over 2m4f at Wexford last time.
runners.comment: Fourth in first two handicaps and only beaten around 3l over 2m4f at Wexford last time

runners.name: Mary's Pride
runners.spotlight: Best form is over 2m and previous attempts over around 2m4f haven't worked out; can go well fresh which he again demonstrated at Ballinrobe, but not sure to back that up.
runners.comment: Can go well fresh which he again demonstrated at Ballinrobe, but not sure to back that up

runners.name: Lord Ffrench
runners.spotlight: Won modest 2m5f maiden in Sligo last summer; well beaten in two handicap starts, but probably needed return; rider has had a fine year in point-to-points.
runners.comment: Won modest 2m5f maiden in Sligo last summer; well beaten in two handicap starts

runners.name: Qaasid
runners.spotlight: Five-time Flat winner in Britain; rightly got controversial 2m4f Limerick handicap hurdle in the stewards' room after being badly hampered by the first past the post; finished off well that day and likely drier ground here is a positive.
runners.comment: Rightly got controversial 2m4f Limerick handicap hurdle in the stewards' room; up 6lb
runners.owner: Four Fools & A Fast Horse Syndicate

runners.name: Costanuci
runners.spotlight: Three-time hurdle winner in 2023; couple of good runs over fences last summer; poor efforts over hurdles last two starts and off since November.
runners.comment: Three-time hurdle winner in 2023; poor efforts last two starts and off since November

runners.name: Digby
runners.spotlight: Has become hard to predict but travelled a bit better than he often does when fifth in a first-time visor at Ballinrobe; cheekpieces back on now and trip looks too sharp.
runners.comment: Fifth in a first-time visor at Ballinrobe; cheekpieces back on and trip looks too sharp

runners.name: Cock And Bull
runners.spotlight: Non-runner.
runners.comment: Non-runner
runners.owner: Sarah Dale & Wayne Dale & Ian Foster

runners.name: Act Of God
runners.spotlight: Old boy had a good career across the codes for the late Edward O'Grady; had been out of action since summer of 2023 until resuming in point-to-points last month.
runners.comment: Out of action since summer of 2023 until resuming in point-to-points last month

runners.name: Fenway Park
runners.spotlight: In receipt of plenty of weight from Qaasid when beating that rival in claimer at Clonmel in February; remote second in another claimer a week later; headgear removed for stable debut.
runners.comment: Got plenty of weight from Qaasid when beating him in claimer at Clonmel; stable debut

runners.name: Goin'
runners.spotlight: Fair performer on the Flat (rated 68) and his latest two runs in that sphere have been fine; experienced rider on board for handicap debut .
runners.comment: Fair performer on the Flat (rated 68) and experienced rider on board for handicap debut

races.race_name: Lily & Wild Mares Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Crossbar Getaway
runners.spotlight: Some okay runs in bumpers last summer and ran her best race over hurdles back from a break at Clonmel (2m1f, good to yielding) last time when a 1.25l second of 18; big player if staying this new trip and finding improvement from that first run back.
runners.comment: Ran her best race over hurdles on seasonal return last time; big player up in trip

runners.name: Some Pretender
runners.spotlight: Debut Galway festival bumper winner last season before running solid races for third in three subsequent Listed bumpers without showing much progress; tailed off in a Grade 3 contest here at the festival most recently; makes hurdling debut in a weak grade and her win came on good ground; bred to appreciate this trip; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Debut Galway festival bumper winner makes hurdling debut in a weak contest; shortlisted

runners.name: Julie Liath
runners.spotlight: Veteran mare made the breakthrough on her seventh point-to-point start at Dromahane in April and backed that up with a third of 16 at Killarney (2m7f, good to yielding) on hurdles bow last time; place chance.
runners.comment: Veteran mare won a point before taking third on hurdles bow last time; place chance

runners.name: Prophetess Miriam
runners.spotlight: Front-runner showed promise in a point and when runner-up in two bumpers; may improve on tailed-off finish at Killarney on hurdles bow when returning from a break; hood goes on.
runners.comment: Promise in a point and bumpers; may improve over hurdle bow when returning from break

runners.name: Working Away
runners.spotlight: One of two for trainer; point winner in 2022 and subsequently bought for 330,000GBP; not seen again until November 2025 when beaten 47l in a 2m4f Cork maiden hurdle (yielding); a bit better later that month at Tramore; back from another break now with a bit to prove.
runners.comment: Point winner has had an interrupted career; bit to prove now back from another break

runners.name: Between Friends
runners.spotlight: Took runner-up spot at Kilbeggan on second hurdle start (2m1f, yielding) and caught the eye of the stewarsd at last year's festival here ; tailed off at Naas in November and pulled up at Cork (2m4f, soft to heavy) on handicap bow; this is weak, so she's in the mix, but others preferred.
runners.comment: Poor in both hurdle starts last term but this is weak so in the mix

runners.name: Bold Reflection
runners.spotlight: Placed in two points and won a bumper in 2022; returned from almost three years off the track at Hexham when pulled up; just 7-1 that day, so perhaps she can come on for it.
runners.comment: Returned from almost three years away when PU at Hexham; maybe she can come on for it

runners.name: Royal Order
runners.spotlight: Dam 2m4f hurdle winner, half-sister to 2m-2m2f hurdle/chase winner Benefit Run, out of 2m/2m1f chase winner; unlikely to make an immediate impact.
runners.comment: Dam 2m4f hurdle winner; unlikely to make an immediate impact.

runners.name: Hello Below
runners.spotlight: 0-7 in points but runner-up in her last two outings; makes hurdling debut now for top point-to-point handler.
runners.comment: 0-7 in points but runner-up in last two outings; makes hurdling bow

runners.name: Fennors Rose
runners.spotlight: Jukebox Jury filly; unsold for 10,500euros as foal; half-sister to six winners inc Fennor Cross (1m-10.3f Flat, RPR 87; useful 2m-2m4f hurdle), Dune Buggy (Italian 8.5f-9.5f) and Infinity Focus (Italian 7.5f-11.4f inc 2yo); dam unraced half-sister to useful 1m2f winner Nurture; likely best watched.
runners.comment: Jukebox Jury filly; half-sister to six winners but likely best watched for her debut.

runners.name: Barstool Prophet
runners.spotlight: Purchased for 30,000euros as a 3yo; Crystal Ocean filly showed nothing on bumper debut here and needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: Crystal Ocean filly showed nothing on bumper debut; big step forward needed

runners.name: Free A Minute
runners.spotlight: Winless from eight point-to-point starts; some credit in her Cork bumper run, although well beaten; a lot of improvement required.
runners.comment: 0-8 in points and well beaten in a bumper; a lot of improvement required

runners.name: Latopix
runners.spotlight: Won over 1m4f in France; modest form in two bumper starts in Britain; didn't build on promising hurdling debut third at Gowran (2m, heavy) when tailed off at Navan in December (2m, soft to heavy).
runners.comment: French 1m4f bumper winner; didn't build on promising hurdles debut when tailed off in Dec'

runners.name: Likeyouhaveplenty
runners.spotlight: Poor jumping on show when pulled up at Tramore; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Poor jumping on show when pulled up at Tramore; hard to fancy

runners.name: Yolanda's Star
runners.spotlight: Tenth of 15 on debut at Downpatrick when 100-1; not totally devoid of promise, but not appealing at the same time.
runners.comment: Tenth of 15 on debut at 100-1; not appealing.

runners.name: Santa Paula
runners.spotlight: Beat just one rival in two bumpers and fell in a point-to-point back from a 641-day absence last time; unlikely.
runners.comment: Beat just one rival in two bumpers and fell in a point back from an absence last time

runners.name: Tenacious Spirit
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in two bumpers; two hurdle runs indicate she will need a handicap mark to have winning prospects.
runners.comment: Runs to date suggests a h'cap may be her best chance of gaining a victory

runners.name: Bossy Bird
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in a Fairyhouse bumper and a 2m4f maiden hurdle at Bellewstown; got to within 27l of the winner at Roscommon last time; others preferred.
runners.comment: Well beaten in three maiden hurdles; others preferred

runners.name: Cherry Red
