runners.spotlight: Maiden pointer has been tailed off in all starts under rules and failed to complete two point-to-points since returning from long absence.
runners.comment: Maiden pointer has failed to complete two points since return from long absence

races.race_name: John Dowling Memorial Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Le Questionnaire
runners.spotlight: Placed in both points and runner-up over hurdles in his last two starts; finished clear of the remainder at Tramore and shaped as though this trip could get more from him when keeping on over 2m3f at Downpatrick last time; ground suits; big player.
runners.comment: Runner-up over hurdles last twice and shaped like this trip would suit; big player

runners.name: Will Wilde
runners.spotlight: Looked promising when winning a Sligo bumper in 2023; pulled up back from 501 days away over 3m here last month (17-2); cheekpieces worn that day now left off.
runners.comment: 2023 Sligo bumper winner PU back from absence here last month

runners.name: Jerry From Kerry
runners.spotlight: Ballinrobe bumper winner for Emmet Mullins and top-rated of these; best of four hurdle runs came on penultimate at Killarney last month when an 11l fourth but didn't build on that back at scene of his win when making some jumping errors; can't be dismissed.
runners.comment: Bumper winner didn't build on decent fourth last time when well beaten; not dismissed

runners.name: Captain Hanley
runners.spotlight: 0-18 on Flat but promising introduction to hurdling at Clonmel when staying on well to take the runner-up spot despite suffering interference (2m4f, good to yielding) and the winner easily followed up next time; unseated at the second when saddle slipped at Kilbeggan; worth another chance, although trip is an unknown.
runners.comment: 0-18 on Flat but made a promising hurdles debut when second; UR latest; worth another go

runners.name: Talladega
runners.spotlight: Failed to complete all three points; impossible to fancy.
runners.comment: Failed to complete all three points; impossible to fancy

runners.name: C Pas Possible
runners.spotlight: 16,000euros 3yo; fourth foal; half-brother to smart 2m hurdle/2m4f chase winner Lombron; dam unraced half-sister to French 1m7f hurdle winner Omessa Has, out of half-sister to Listed-placed 1m4f Flat and useful 2m-2m4f hurdle winner Enfant De Lune; last of five finishers on point debut last month.
runners.comment: Half-brother to smart hurdle/chase winner Lombron; last of five finishers on point bow

runners.name: Les Issards
runners.spotlight: 21,000euros 2yo, 20,000euros 3yo, and bought for 28,000euros in April following his third-place finish in his first point-to-point; dam 1m3f-1m4f AQPS Flat winner, half-sister to winners Vieux Frere (French 2m1f-2m5f hurdle/chase) and As Tu Vu (1m4f AQPS Flat); check market for rules bow.
runners.comment: Purchased for 28,000euros after finishing third in sole point; check market for rules bow

runners.name: Ajp Falco
runners.spotlight: No impact in bumpers; beaten when fell on hurdling bow at Clonmel and pulled up at Listowel last time.
runners.comment: Beaten when fell on first hurdles run and PU at Listowel last time

runners.name: Glenary Prince
runners.spotlight: Tailed off on hurdling debut at Fairyhouse in April (2m4f, yielding to soft); in good hands but may need more time.
runners.comment: Tailed off on debut; in good hands but may need more time

runners.name: Raleagh Scope
runners.spotlight: Never a factor in four starts under rules last year nor in five points since February; 29l behind at Downpatrick last time; new headgear tried but hard to make a case for him.
runners.comment: Never a factor in points nor starts under rules to date; hard to make a case for

runners.name: Maison De Balzac
runners.spotlight: Experienced point-to-pointer; 1-10 in that sphere for Colin Bowe; tailed off on rules bow last time; unlikely.
runners.comment: 1-10 in points and tailed off on rules bow last time; unlikely.

runners.name: Little Big John
runners.spotlight: Pulled up and tailed off at Fairyhouse and Clonmel on first two starts; hood reached for; well held in a similar race at this course yesterday.
runners.comment: Pulled up and tailed off on first two starts; hood on now

races.race_name: TRM Supporting Every Stride Of The Journey Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: William Tell
runners.spotlight: Maiden and 2m6f novice winner last year represents a stable in fine form and will be better for last month's Killarney return.
runners.comment: Maiden and 2m6f novice winner last year and will be better for Killarney return

runners.name: Accustomed
runners.spotlight: Best form is over 2m4f, inclduing when runner-up in Down Royal handicap in March; has run poorly since and tries longer trip here with headgear removed; did win a soft-ground point over 3m.
runners.comment: Second over 2m4f in Down Royal handicap but has run poorly since; tries longer trip here

runners.name: Morell River
runners.spotlight: Both wins have come over 2m4f at Wexford, including on return from layoff last month; stayers in family, including Mikael D'Haguenet, and it all hinges on whether she'll get the trip.
runners.comment: Both wins have come over 2m4f at Wexford, including on return from layoff last month

runners.name: Jackmoon
runners.spotlight: Still searching for a first win but had some good bumper form early on and hasn't done a lot wrong over hurdles; went close in 2m7f maiden in April and solid third over 3m at Wexford when favourite for handicap debut; should be thereabouts with 7lb claimer back on.
runners.comment: Stays well and solid third over 3m when favourite for handicap debut; might get his turn

runners.name: Maverick Mack
runners.spotlight: Touched off in a point on nice ground; maiden winner over 2m on heavy; well held over 2m4f next time and hasn't got far in two starts this year.
runners.comment: Maiden winner over 2m, well held over 2m4f next time; not got far in two starts this year

runners.name: Juke Hill
runners.spotlight: Some fair runs in maidens and heavy ground probably to blame when well-beaten joint-favourite on handicap debut in January; back from a break.
runners.comment: Heavy ground probably to blame when well-beaten favourite on handicap debut in January

runners.name: Smackwater Jack
runners.spotlight: Won a division of this last year off 92 and he's higher here as a result of being 13lb wrong; well beaten in recent starts.
runners.comment: Won a division of this last year off 92 and he's higher here because of being 13lb wrong

runners.name: Good World
runners.spotlight: Latest of five wins was at Fairyhouse in November; pulled up over fences last time; handles good ground and has won over 3m; trainer has said that once he goes over mark of 100, he struggles, but claim will help.
runners.comment: Latest of five wins was at Fairyhouse in November; pulled up over fences last time

runners.name: Work Place
runners.spotlight: Beaten not less than 40l all starts, so the fitting of headgear is the only hope.
runners.comment: Has been beaten not less than 40l all starts, so the fitting of headgear is the only hope

runners.name: Ottoman Style
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced since winning back-to-back 2m4f handicaps in 2023; after six months off, his Kilbeggan comeback was encouraging and he's on a good mark if he comes forward from that.
runners.comment: Lightly raced since winning back-to-back 2m4f handicaps in 2023; comeback was encouraging

runners.name: Falcon Eight
runners.spotlight: Classy stayer on the Flat but getting long in the tooth; infrequent runner over hurdles nowadays and was well behind when unseating late on at the festival here.
runners.comment: Classy stayer on the Flat but getting long in the tooth; well behind when unseating latest
runners.owner: Mark E Phelan & S P O'Connor

runners.name: Beyond Your Dreams
runners.spotlight: Flat and 2m maiden hurdle winner was only beaten 10l in last year's Fred Winter, but has struggled since; while going a longer trip in first-time blinkers wouldn't be everyone's cup of tea, perhaps the trainer knows better.
runners.comment: Flat and maiden hurdle winner has been struggling in recent handicap starts; longer trip

runners.name: Streets Of Doyen
runners.spotlight: Seven-time winner but best years are behind him and didn't show much in points last term; stable debut.
runners.comment: Seven-time winner but best years are behind him and didn't show much in points last term

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Mares (Pro/Am) INH Flat Race

runners.name: Al Arrivee
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to Cheltenham Gold Cup winner Al Boum Photo won at Sligo last summer (good) before finishing second against some useful geldings at Galway; reappeared at the festival here in April when not far off the placed horses in Grade 3 won impressively by a stablemate; plenty of decent mares behind her then.
runners.comment: Not far off the placed horses in C&D Grade 3 won impressively by a stablemate

runners.name: Coco's Legacy
runners.spotlight: Well beaten on debut but only 8-1 at Downpatrick (2m2f, good) where she made all with Elliott and Mullins supplying the placed horses.
runners.comment: Made all at Downpatrick (2m2f, good) with Elliott and Mullins supplying the placed horses

runners.name: Brave Lady
runners.spotlight: Good start when placed at Galway last summer; 66-1 when outclassed in Aintree Grade 2 in April; 6-4 at Limerick when beaten by market rival who looks a useful prospect.
runners.comment: Placed twice including latest when beaten by newcomer who looks a useful prospect

runners.name: False Dawn
runners.spotlight: Ballylinch-bred Make Believe filly cost E35,000 as a yearling; second foal; dam unraced half-sister to 7f/1m winner Kinver Edge, out of US 1m Grade 3 winning sister to US 1m Grade 1 winner Jack Milton; unraced for Ken Condon.
runners.comment: Ballylinch-bred Make Believe filly cost E35,000 as a yearling; unraced for Ken Condon

runners.name: Saoirses Choice
runners.spotlight: Fair start at Limerick recently when fourth of 8, but was over 11l off Brave Lady then.
runners.comment: Fair start at Limerick recently when fourth of eight, but was over 11l off Brave Lady then

runners.name: Are You There
runners.spotlight: Closely related to winners Hardwired (2m1f-2m7f hurdle/chase) and Be Fierce (1m7f chase/2m hurdle); half-sister to dual bumper and maiden hurdle winner Friary Road; dam bumper (inc Listed) and 2m/2m2f hurdle winner, out of unraced sister to high-class French 1m-10.5f winner Act One; late debut for daughter of Pour Moi.
runners.comment: From a family the trainer has done well with; late debut for daughter of Pour Moi

runners.name: Hope And Mercy
runners.spotlight: Idaho mare out of unraced half-sister to smart 2m-2m5f hurdle/chase winner Go Roger Go; 40s for Galway festival bumper, towards rear when losing her rider 4f out; tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: 40s for Galway festival bumper, towards rear when losing her rider 4f out; tongue-tie

runners.name: Any News For Me
runners.spotlight: Wasn't been beaten out the gate by any means in winter bumpers at Leopardstown and Navan, but unlikely to contend all the same.
runners.comment: Not shamed in winter bumpers at Leopardstown and Navan, but unlikely to contend

runners.name: Freya Cova
runners.spotlight: From a good family and is sister to smart bumper performer Windbeneathmywings; tailed off on debut at Down Royal; hood.
runners.comment: Sister to smart bumper performer Windbeneathmywings; tailed off at Down Royal; hood

runners.name: Big Budgie
runners.spotlight: 50s, beaten 33l in Kilbeggan bumper over a year ago.
runners.comment: 50s, beaten 33l in Kilbeggan bumper over a year ago

races.race_name: Scone Palace Jousting Tournament Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Trigg
runners.spotlight: Did well in bumpers and comfortably off the mark over hurdles at Uttoxeter (2m, good); this is tougher than that winnable race but he's well related and entitled to improve plenty more with racing.
runners.comment: Did well in bumpers and comfortably off the mark over hurdles at Uttoxeter

runners.name: Kibris
runners.spotlight: Useful Flat winner for Joseph O'Brien; 0-4 over hurdles for that yard but came good on stable debut (same ownership) with a narrow win over 2m here last month (good to soft); he would have won easier but for a late mistake; there are some stamina concerns over quite this far.
runners.comment: Useful Flat winner; won over 2m here last month; some stamina concerns at this trip

runners.name: Flying Ace
runners.spotlight: Only his second run over hurdles when making no mistake at odds-on in a good-ground 2m1f maiden at Cartmel 11 days ago; the RPR was modest but he did beat a useful Flat recruit and his yard has won three of the last four renewals of this race.
runners.comment: Cartmel winner from a yard that's won three of the last four renewals of this race

runners.name: Dolly Dior
runners.spotlight: Insufficient promise in two good-ground bumpers to warrant a second look here on hurdle debut.
runners.comment: Insufficient promise in two good-ground bumpers to warrant a second look on hurdle debut

runners.name: Saint Cunning
runners.spotlight: Ordinary form when fifth in a bumper here but did go off only 5-1 and he raced freely before weakening late on; could improve on that but clearly needs to.
runners.comment: Ordinary form when fifth in a bumper here but did go off only 5-1

runners.name: Busted Springs
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in bumpers at Ayr (heavy) and here (good to soft); huge odds both times.
runners.comment: Tailed off in bumpers at Ayr (heavy) and here (good to soft); huge odds both times

runners.name: Brewdog
runners.spotlight: 300-1 when tailed off behind Kibris here 24 days ago; no appeal.
runners.comment: 300-1 when tailed off behind Kibris here 24 days ago; no appeal

races.race_name: Sun Racing The Home Of Racing Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: King Kodiak
runners.spotlight: Placed off 5lb higher at Carlisle (2m3f, good to soft) in April but has very mixed record over hurdles and is sometimes a clumsy jumper; safely held on last two outings; others look much safer.
runners.comment: Placed off 5lb higher in April but has very mixed record over hurdles and comes with risk

runners.name: Magic Gloves
runners.spotlight: Promising fourth on handicap debut at Musselburgh (1m7f, soft) in December but suffered heavy defeats on final two starts last season, the latter over C&D, and needs subsequent wind op to have made a big difference.
runners.comment: Made promising handicap debut but two heavy defeats followed; had wind op in April

runners.name: Bouboule
runners.spotlight: Placed in Musselburgh maiden (1m7f, good to soft) in November but has looked out of sorts over hurdles and on the Flat in recent months (had wind op before latest run); needs to turn a corner.
runners.comment: Out of form over hurdles and on the Flat in recent months; needs to turn a corner

runners.name: Morandi Second
runners.spotlight: 0-11 over hurdles and absent since weakening fifth at Uttoxeter (2m, good) last summer; current mark is workable but others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: 0-11 over hurdles and absent since weakening fifth at Uttoxeter last summer

runners.name: Breadalbane Lass
runners.spotlight: Kept on too strongy for Storm The Dug over C&D (good) in April, getting off the mark over hurdles at the 12th attempt, but was well held (albeit on soft ground) at Hexham last month; others have more potential.
runners.comment: Good C&D winner in April, her 12th hurdle run; well held since; others have more potential

runners.name: Allibaba
runners.spotlight: Returned from long absence with good-ground wins at Market Rasen and Warwick for new stable last month; should cope if the ground is softer here and might still be on the upgrade; must be considered.
runners.comment: Back from long absence with two wins for new stable last month; could have more to offer

runners.name: Struth
runners.spotlight: Useful on the Flat in his prime but well held when fifth on handicap hurdle debut at Kelso (2m2f, good; first-time tongue-tie) in April and showed no significant improvement in a subsequent C&D maiden; needs to up his game.
runners.comment: Well held on handicap debut in April, and showed no improvement in C&D maiden last month

runners.name: Storm The Dug
runners.spotlight: Fair Flat racer (albeit a maiden) who kept on from the rear for clear second behind Breadalbane Lass over C&D (good) on handicap hurdle debut in April; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Kept on from the rear for second over C&D on handicap hurdle debut in April; shortlisted

runners.name: Betteryouthanme
runners.spotlight: Displayed improvement and travelled well for a long way when third here (2m4f, good to soft) on last month's handicap debut; may benefit from this drop back in trip; one to consider.
runners.comment: Travelled well when third over 2m4f here on handicap debut; drops back in trip today

runners.name: Karthala
runners.spotlight: Over 8l behind Alibaba when third at Warwick (2m, good; refitted cheekpieces) last month but that was still a pretty good start to his handicap hurdle career and further progress is possible.
runners.comment: Good start to handicap hurdle career when placed behind Alibaba at Warwick last month

runners.name: Bray Dale
runners.spotlight: Struggled in Hexham seller last month (first-time visor) and is now 0-10 over hurdles; 2lb out of the weights here; difficult to enthuse over.
runners.comment: Struggled in Hexham seller last month and is now 0-10 over hurdles

runners.name: Jack Dempsey
runners.spotlight: Returned from break with fairly encouraging run in C&D novice in April but was pulled up in 2m4f handicap here last month and has a fair bit more to prove than some of these.
runners.comment: Pulled up here (2m4f) on last month's handicap debut; drops back in trip today

runners.name: Blondina
runners.spotlight: Showed no significant promise in her three qualifying races over hurdles in January-March but makes handicap hurdle debut from a basement mark and returns to this code after winning on the Flat (1m4f; RPR 61) last month; interesting.
runners.comment: Has no form over hurdles but won on the Flat last month; interesting off basement mark

races.race_name: Scone Palace International Horse Trials Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Scots Poet
runners.spotlight: Exposed multiple winner; he would have found the ground too soft when beaten by King Sacre at Hexham and then dug deep to win at Kelso (2m, good; 5lb lower); entitled to have some say.
runners.comment: Recent Kelso winner who is entitled to have some say off 5lb higher

runners.name: Just Dottie
runners.spotlight: Fourth of six on the Flat last time out and beaten 22l here the time before that; a four-time winner in this discipline but reservations at the minute.
runners.comment: Fourth of six on the Flat last time out and beaten 22l here the time before that

runners.name: Scriabin
runners.spotlight: 0-3 on the Flat and 0-13 over hurdles, having had a few chances now in maidens and handicaps; has to raise his game.
runners.comment: 0-13 over hurdles, having had a few chances now in maidens and handicaps

runners.name: King Sacre
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in his first handicap (on good) and then made all the running on soft ground at Hexham, beating Scots Poet by some 17l; raised 9lb for that but he's far from exposed.
runners.comment: Made all for an easy win at Hexham; raised 9lb for that but he's far from exposed

runners.name: Pure Gold
runners.spotlight: Progressed well last year but the signs are that she might be a shade high in the weights now after heavy defeats here since April.
runners.comment: A shade high in the weights now after heavy defeats here since April
runners.owner: Thp Racing & D A Harrison Racing

runners.name: Ravenscraig Castle
runners.spotlight: Good fourth at Musselburgh in March but tailed off in his last run over hurdles and down the field on the Flat last week; others look more likely.
runners.comment: Tailed off in his last run over hurdles and down the field on the Flat last week
runners.owner: A Sinclair, I Jardine & V Renwick

runners.name: Cosmic Soul
runners.spotlight: His hat-trick (2m1f-2m4f; good) has come at a cost mark-wise but it was all very straightforward last time at Sedgefield; something has clicked big time and he could deal with another 6lb.
runners.comment: On a four-timer; something has clicked big time and he could deal with another 6lb
runners.owner: M Lee, D Lee, C Jolley & Neville

runners.name: Ailt An Chorrain
runners.spotlight: Record of 2-6 over hurdles, though neither win came in a handicap; only one effort of note in five runs over fences and blinkers are replaced by cheekpieces; the stable probably has brighter prospects with Cosmic Soul.
runners.comment: Has been chasing and the stable probably has brighter prospects with Cosmic Soul

runners.name: Tigress Lilly
runners.spotlight: In a good yard and returns from wind surgery, but she was 50-1 for her handicap debut here in April and performed accordingly.
runners.comment: 50-1 for handicap debut here in April and performed accordingly; had a wind op

runners.name: Mr Lincoln
runners.spotlight: 0-9 over hurdles but placed in his last two attempts and went very close at Carlisle in March (2m1f, good to soft); Thursday's Flat run when sixth wasn't too shoddy; can go well.
runners.comment: Placed in last two hurdles races; Thursday's Flat run when sixth wasn't too shoddy a run

runners.name: Largy Meadow
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in the two handicaps he has completed and could have done with a truer pace when chasing home the market leader over 2m4f here last time; he's highly likely to get one here and he's one to consider.
runners.comment: Runner-up in the two handicaps he has completed; truer pace back at 2m can suit

runners.name: Kinbara Firstdraft
runners.spotlight: Won this two years ago and later added another handicap off this mark; however, he has rather lost his way and now kicks off for a new yard; enjoys good ground.
runners.comment: Won this in 2024; he has rather lost his way and now kicks off for a new yard

runners.name: Alidam
runners.spotlight: Ex-French gelding; beaten 6l on handicap debut at Huntingdon (2m, soft) but failed to fire at Sedgefield in the new cheekpieces that are retained; others preferred.
runners.comment: Failed to fire at Sedgefield in the new cheekpieces that are retained

races.race_name: Perth Silver Cup Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Hypotenus
runners.spotlight: Free-goer who often makes the running; well beaten when last of three on debut for Neil Mulholland in April but last month's third of seven behind Newtown Rambler over C&D (good to soft) was more encouraging, and he may yet have more to offer for his new stable.
runners.comment: Well beaten on stable debut but recent C&D third behind Newtown Rambler was much better

runners.name: Garde Des Champs
runners.spotlight: Has modest strike-rate (2-41 under all codes) and was behind Newtown Rambler and Hypotenus when fourth of seven over C&D good to soft) last month but is now 1lb lower than for last summer's convincing C&D (good) win, and can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Behind two of these when fourth here last month but won off 1lb higher over C&D last year

runners.name: Captain Cool
runners.spotlight: Launched chasing career with wins at Stratford (2m1f, good to firm) and Plumpton (2m1f, good) last month, and also ran well when second over hurdles on Tuesday; evidently in very good nick but current chase mark asks a question of him, and today's forecast slower ground is probably not ideal.
runners.comment: 2-2 over fences (both wins in May); ran well over hurdles on Tuesday; ground a worry here

runners.name: Classic Maestro
runners.spotlight: Battled on well to win gruelling contest here (2m4f, good) in April; that form is treated with a degree of caution but he's only 2lb higher today and this shorter trip on forecast slower ground will suit.
runners.comment: Battled on well to win gruelling 2m4f contest here in April; 2m on slower ground will suit

runners.name: Newtown Rambler
runners.spotlight: Bounced back to form with hurdle win in first-time cheekpieces/refitted tongue-tie in March, his second start for Adrian Keatley, and made all over C&D (good to soft) when back over fences last month; obvious contender.
runners.comment: Won over hurdles in March and made all over C&D when back over fences last month respected

runners.name: Itwasfate
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish 9yo who is 1-5 in British points this year; ran out on latest appearance three weeks ago and it's hard to tell whether he can still be competitive off this sort of mark under rules.
runners.comment: Won point in April but it's hard to tell whether he can still be competitive at this level

races.race_name: Perth Gold Cup Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Breizh River
runners.spotlight: Career bests on the RPR front in his last two races, winning at Kelso (2m5f) and close up at Aintree (2m4f; again on good); could run well, though a nagging concern that he didn't appear to stay 3m when he tried it once before (at this track).
runners.comment: In form but nagging concern that he didn't appear to stay 3m when he tried it once before

runners.name: Stans The Man
runners.spotlight: On a hat-trick when a fairly close third in this last year and he returns off a 4lb lower mark, and in a solid run of form; enters the reckoning.
runners.comment: On a hat-trick when a close third in this last year and he returns off a 4lb lower mark

runners.name: Riskintheground
runners.spotlight: Good ground is key to him, though good to soft is acceptable; after signs of a revival at the Cheltenham Festival he duly delivered in a competitive 2m4f handicap at Haydock, winning cosily despite narrow margin of victory; the 4lb rise seemed generous and when beaten at Uttoxeter he bumped into the talented Glengouly on a good day; all his best form is at shorter, so stamina would be the principal concern.
runners.comment: Back in the good books and would have every chance if proving his stamina at 3m

runners.name: Walk On Quest
runners.spotlight: Made it 5-12 over fences when readily taking care of two rivals over a similar trip at Kelso a month ago (good; made most); only 3lb higher but without Jack Power's 5lb claim, so at this level he's probably in need of his best form yet.
runners.comment: 5-12 over fences after his Kelso win; at this level probably needs his best form yet

runners.name: Somespring Special
runners.spotlight: Has enhanced her good strike-rate over fences with back-to-back wins at Newton Abbot (2m5f) and Bangor (3m; again on good); it was harder work last time after a 6lb rise and she now has another 5lb to contend with, though last time was her first go over this far.
runners.comment: Up the weights for recent wins but last time was her first go at 3m

runners.name: Light N Strike
runners.spotlight: Back from 193 days off when a 16l fourth to Somespring Special at Newton Abbot (2m5f) and ran as though that race would bring him on; that was his best effort yet since leaving Emma Lavelle, for whom he was competitive off higher marks; trip/ground fine.
runners.comment: Well treated on form for previous yard and signs of a revival last time at Newton Abbot

runners.name: Duffle Coat
runners.spotlight: Only 1-20 over fences, the win coming in the 3m Mayo National in 2024 (good ground); finished a fine second in the Galway Plate that summer but he's been underperforming for a while now and belatedly tries a tongue-tie.
runners.comment: He's been underperforming for a while now and belatedly tries a tongue-tie

runners.name: Statuario
runners.spotlight: Seeking a hat-trick in this race and on the back of a seventh course success in April when he dead-heated over this trip; not handicapped out of it after just a 3lb rise, for all that he's never defied this high a mark in the past.
runners.comment: Seeking a hat-trick in this race and on the back of a seventh course success in April

runners.name: The Real Whacker
runners.spotlight: Now well handicapped judged on peak efforts, but his form during the last 12 months lacks substance; pulled up in the Grand National last time on his return to fences.
runners.comment: Reduced mark but his form during the last 12 months lacks substance

runners.name: Magna Sam
runners.spotlight: Won the Edinburgh National over an extended 3m7f at Musselburgh in 2023 and 2025 (both good to soft); has not recaptured his form in his last three races and now returns from further wind surgery; 6lb out of the weights.
runners.comment: Back from wind surgery; ideally wants further and he's 6lb out of the weights

races.race_name: Malcolm Group Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Inis Oirr
runners.spotlight: Generally disappointing since winning valuable marathon in February 2024 but won off much-reduced mark over C&D (good) in April and also ran well when second behind a progressive rival here last month; considered.
runners.comment: Won off much-reduced mark over C&D in April and again ran well here last month

runners.name: Sir Carnegie
runners.spotlight: Placed twice over C&D before keeping on well to win three-finisher contest here (2m4f, good to soft) last month; fourth win over fences came with first-time blinkers (instead of cheekpieces) at Hexham (3m) yesterday evening when he got up close home; respected if he turns out quickly again.
runners.comment: Won here (2m4f) last month and got up close home at Hexham (3m; blinkers) yesterday

runners.name: Ira Hayes
runners.spotlight: Almost certainly fortunate when winning Tramore beginners' chase (2m5f, good to yielding) in April, given he was in fourth (albeit close up) when the front three came to grief two out, but ran big race when clear second in Market Rasen handicap (3m, good) last month and features prominently in calculations here.
runners.comment: Ran big race when clear second at Market Rasen last month; commands respect here

runners.name: Neon Diamond
runners.spotlight: Yet to make any further progress since easily winning Kilarney maiden hurdle (2m7f, yielding to soft) in first-time cheekpieces last summer and was well held when upped to 3m2f for handicap chase debut in April (her fourth run over fences); that said, her stable's runners are always respected here.
runners.comment: Well held on handicap chase debut in April but stable runners always respected here

runners.name: Wasdell Dundalk
runners.spotlight: Veteran who gained four of his six chase wins over C&D and is versatile regarding ground; well beaten when 50-1 for 2m4f hurdle here last month, after a layoff, but that was also the case before he bounced back to form and finished second in this race a year ago; can't be ignored.
runners.comment: Four-time C&D winner; probably needed last month's hurdle run; can do much better today
runners.owner: Abbadis Racing & D A Harrison Racing

runners.name: Buy Some Time
runners.spotlight: Raced exclusively over hurdles since finishing second in Cartmel hunter chase on rules debut in spring 2024; caused 50-1 shock at 2025 Punchestown festival and has been placed twice in today's headgear combination here this spring (2m4f/3m2f); contender if able to transfer that form to fences.
runners.comment: Placed twice over hurdles here this spring; contender if that form transfers to fences

runners.name: Settle Down Jill
runners.spotlight: Looked in very good nick when readily winning Kelso hunter chase (2m7f, good) for his owner-trainer last month but faces quite daunting assignment off career-high mark on this return to a handicap; now in the care of Tristan Davidson.
runners.comment: Won Kelso hunter chase in good style last month but faces tougher task here

races.race_name: IM Group Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Watchoutitscookie
runners.spotlight: 2m4f hurdle and 2m7f chase winner (once each) from 35 races; his last win was in 2024 but bits of form this year, including penultimate start, give him an each-way chance.
runners.comment: Last won in 2024 but bits of 2026 form, including penultimate start, give each-way chance

runners.name: Creadan Grace
runners.spotlight: Upped to 3m for a last-gasp win in 20-runner race at Down Royal in October; respectable fourth of 18 under James Smith at Punchestown (again 3m on good to yielding) last month confirms her in serious calculations.
runners.comment: Last-gasp 3m win at Down Royal in October and fourth of 18 at Punchestown last month

runners.name: Stiletto
runners.spotlight: Won three times (2m2f-3m) in June-September last year, off today's mark over C&D on last occasion; out of form on last four outings, however, and a major revival is needed on this second start after wind surgery; tongue-tie (last used in 2024) returns.
runners.comment: Three wins in June-September last year (C&D latest) but out of form on last four outings

runners.name: Burgundy Man
runners.spotlight: 0-20 over hurdles but won here (3m, soft) on chase debut last October; three major backward steps this spring prompt caution on this return to hurdling, with cheekpieces (tried often as hurdler last year) reinstated.
runners.comment: 0-20 over hurdles; won chase debut last October; three major backward steps this spring

runners.name: Leader Wing
runners.spotlight: Won a bumper in May 2024; 0-15 as a hurdler for Gillian Boanas but had his share of seconds and thirds, including over 3m; £12,500 sale in March was quickly followed by wind surgery and, although usual headgear is dispensed with, new trainer Dan Skelton has something to work with; one to note.
runners.comment: 0-15 hurdling for Gillian Boanas but with some 2nds and 3rds; player on Skelton debut

runners.name: Jakana
runners.spotlight: Partial revival on chase debut here (3m, good) in April but she did not shine at Cartmel one month later; has plenty of form to make this look an attractive mark but there are doubts on this return to hurdling.
runners.comment: Plenty of form makes this look an attractive mark, but doubts on this return to hurdling

runners.name: Maillot Blanc
runners.spotlight: Won this race last term and better form this spring, all on good ground; dead-heated for first at Kelso (2m5f) last time, which has pushed him up 3lb to his highest mark since 2024 but he looks much more likely than most of these to give his running; should be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Won this last term and better form this spring, all on good ground; should be thereabouts

runners.name: Reagrove Lord
runners.spotlight: 0-17 as hurdler but plenty of minor honours, including C&D; needs to improve upon last month's fourth at Hexham but that was his first run for 400 days, so he may well build on that; case can be made off career-low mark.
runners.comment: 0-17 hurdling but plenty of minor honours; second run after 400 days off; case can be made

runners.name: Breaking Ground
runners.spotlight: This is her handicap debut and a major step up in trip, but there have been signs of only a little ability; tongue tied on latest start and a hood (tried four starts back, pulled up) is reinstated.
runners.comment: This is her handicap debut and a major step up in trip, but signs of only a little ability

runners.name: Myfavouritesister
runners.spotlight: 13-race maiden, with nine of those races over hurdles; did little on chase debut last time (after six months off) but several bits of hurdle form last year, including over C&D, give her a place chance.
runners.comment: Several bits of hurdle form last year, including over C&D, give her a place chance

runners.name: Shighness
runners.spotlight: No win since a chase early in 2024; only occasional echoes of that form and she was held when unseating on the turn at Hexham on latest start.
runners.comment: No win since a chase early in 2024; only occasional echoes of that form

runners.name: Dalileo
runners.spotlight: This race in 2024 is among his three Perth wins from 23 visits; latest win was last July over C&D again from 2lb higher than today and that was his second run after a break, the same scenario which presents itself today; needs a second look but others are preferred for the win.
runners.comment: This race in 2024 is among his three Perth wins from 23 visits; needs a second look
runners.owner: Thp Racing & D A Harrison Racing

runners.name: Our Girl Sal
runners.spotlight: Got up on the line at Kelso (3m2f) in May 2025 on second start for Paul Robson, opening her rules account; made the running when well beaten behind Maillot Blanc in this race last year, however, and not seen since; has changed hands and now has first run for Adam Nichol, with a tongue-tie (tried twice in 2024) restored.
runners.comment: 3m2f win in May 2025 but absent since well beaten in this race that year; new yard

runners.name: Luminaries
runners.spotlight: Tried chasing last autumn and did not show much back over hurdles in the winter on last two outings; 4-20 overall as a hurdler, with all four wins in March-May 2025, but there have to be questions at the moment.
runners.comment: All four wins in March-May 2025 and there have to be questions at the moment

races.race_name: Belmont Stakes Presented By NYRA Bets (Grade 1) (Dirt)

runners.name: Renegade
runners.spotlight: Impressive winner of Grade 1 Arkansas Derby at Oaklawn, where he changed gear and ran on strongly (a very fast finishing split); neck second to Golden Tempo in Grade 1 Kentucky Derby, bumped early on inside but working through to challenge and running on, no match for winner close home; should give another good account and could be ready for an improved effort after five-week break.
runners.comment: Impressive Grade 1 winner at Oaklawn, touched off in Kentucky Derby; must go well again
runners.owner: Robert Low, Lawana L Low & Repole Stable

runners.name: Golden Tempo
runners.spotlight: Benefited from pace meltdown when winning Grade 1 Kentucky Derby by a nose from Renegade, virtually tailed off early before relentless progress last 3f, then running on strongly to lead close home; has more stamina than his contemporaries but surely needs similar kind of race to put that in play again; trained with great skill by Cherie Devaux.
runners.comment: Came from long way back to win Kentucky Derby by a nose from Renegade; same set-up needed

runners.name: Chief Wallabee
runners.spotlight: Highly talented colt who gives impression he is keeping something back; in frame in Grade 1 events for Florida Derby at Gulfstream Park in March and Kentucky Derby (1m2f) in May; hesitated when short of room at top of straight when 3l fourth to Golden Tempo on latter occasion; should be thereabouts but needs to be braver.
runners.comment: Good 3l fourth to Golden Tempo in Grade 1 Kentucky Derby, short of room; place claims

runners.name: Commandment
runners.spotlight: Admirable colt with a tremendous attitude to racing; fourth consecutive victory when nose winner (from The Puma) of Grade 1 Florida Derby at Gulfstream Park in March, closing well off a slow pace; run best ignored in Kentucky Derby, starting slowly and forced wide in hottest part of race; could easily pick up winning thread, provided earlier races have not left their mark.
runners.comment: Game Grade 1 Florida Derby winner, best forgiven 7th in Kentucky Derby; could bounce back

runners.name: Growth Equity
runners.spotlight: Really likeable colt who is thriving at present; maiden winner at Aqueduct in March before Grade 3 Peter Pan score on same track in May (1m1f) when he subdued very useful Talk To Me Jimmy by 2l while strong at finish; should be suited by the extra furlong and has place claims, if improving again.
runners.comment: Improving 2l Grade 3 Peter Pan winner (1m1f) at Aqueduct last month; place claims

runners.name: Powershift
runners.spotlight: Second favourite for Grade 3 Tampa Bay Derby after promising second (to Emerging Market) in a maiden, but beaten nearly 10l into sixth; impressive in maiden at Churchill Downs (8.5f) last month, drawing nearly 3l clear in taking style (fast time); short of the required standard on form, but should be suited by the longer trip and could outrun his odds from the front.
runners.comment: Confirmed earlier promise in Churchill Downs maiden win, drawing clear; could surprise

runners.name: Emerging Market
runners.spotlight: Remarkable time performance to win maiden at Tampa Bay in February, then tenacious, despite inexperience, when following up in Grade 2 Louisiana Derby (9.5f) at Fair Grounds with Golden Tempo in third; only tenth in Kentucky Derby, close to strong pace and tiring badly in stretch; it was a big ask at Churchill Downs on only third start and he could have learned a good deal from the experience.
runners.comment: Grade 2 Louisiana Derby winner; only 10th in Kentucky Derby, not much room; better to come

runners.name: Vitruvian Man
runners.spotlight: Keeneland maiden winner last October and placed in two allowance races later in 2025; 47-1, best effort when 9.5l third to So Happy in Grade 1 Santa Anita Derby (1m1f) in April, staying on from rear; stamina glutton who will be very much at home over the extra furlong here; minor place prospects.
runners.comment: Outsider when 9.5l third in 1m1f Grade 1 at Santa Anita, staying on; minor place prospects
runners.owner: Glenn Sorgenstein WC Racing Inc & Run Fast Racing

runners.name: Ottinho
runners.spotlight: Game winner of 1m1f maiden at Aqueduct on second start as juvenile; beaten more than 10l and never able to challenge when both third in Listed race there and second to Further Ado in Grade 1 Blue Grass Stakes at Keeneland; stays well but surely needs a destructive pace up front to claim a place.
runners.comment: Beaten more than 10l last two starts when third in Listed and 2nd in Grade 1; lacks pace

races.race_name: Soaring Softly Stakes (Grade 3) (Turf)

runners.name: Kingsolver
runners.owner: Storyteller Racing, Michael Schroeck Et Al

races.race_name: Yasuda Kinen (Grade 1) (Turf)
HKU Faculties Of Arts And Education Handicap (Class 5) (Dirt)

runners.name: Viva Taste
runners.spotlight: 12-race maiden; competitive in higher-grade company in first half of this season but never really looked dangerous when seventh over C&D last month.
runners.comment: 12-race maiden who never really looked dangerous over C&D last month
runners.owner: Jimmy Chung Kwok Man & Dominic Chung Won Lok

runners.name: Golden Fortune
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten over 7f here last month and has now been unplaced all eight turf starts; goes beyond 1m for first time on this dirt debut; needs the new test to enable significant improvement.
runners.comment: Unplaced all eight turf starts; needs significant improvement on this dirt debut

runners.name: Special Hedge
runners.spotlight: Made the frame off much higher mark over C&D in early part of this season but has looked out of sorts in recent months, including when dipping into this grade for latest run.
runners.comment: Well handicapped on early-season form but out of sorts in recent months

runners.name: Fortune Kingo
runners.spotlight: Ten-race maiden; ran quite well on soft turf (1m1f) two starts ago but generally disappointing this year; drops in grade for dirt return.
runners.comment: Ran okay on soft turf two starts ago but generally disappointing this year

runners.name: I Excelle
runners.spotlight: Made fairly encouraging debut here (6f, turf) in December but has finished down the field five times since (6f-1m); upped in trip and dropped in grade for dirt debut.
runners.comment: Down the field all five starts since fairly encouraging debut in December

runners.name: Happydearhappydeer
runners.spotlight: Arrested his decline with respectable fifth here (1m, dirt) and remains very well handicapped on last season's placed form over C&D but he's drawn on the outside today and it's hard to tell whether he'll build on that recent effort.
runners.comment: Latest run arrested his decline but it's hard to tell whether he'll build on it
runners.owner: Hong Yiu, Lynn Fu Lam Wu Et Al

runners.name: General Smart
runners.spotlight: Not very consistent but came close to a breakthrough win here (7f, turf) in March and put two poor runs behind him when fourth (1m, turf) a fortnight ago; ran okay on only previous dirt start; has a fighting chance if in the right mood.
runners.comment: Not very consistent but this year's peak turf form brings him into the equation
runners.owner: Alex Chau Man Lok, Jack Chan Hoi Et Al

runners.name: Meepmeep
runners.spotlight: No longer the force of old but had been consistent for a while before last month's 1m course win; will be fine over today's longer trip; probably won't be far away.
runners.comment: Had been consistent before last month's 1m course win; probably won't be far away

runners.name: Firefoot
runners.spotlight: Has modest 1-29 strike-rate but scores good marks for consistency, and finished second over C&D on his last two outings; nicely drawn on the inside; obvious contender.
runners.comment: Runner-up over C&D on last two outings; obvious contender from stall 1

runners.name: Celestial Harmony
runners.spotlight: Returned to form to make winning stable debut here (1m, dirt) in January and ran at least as well when fourth over that trip in April; below that level on turf later in April but could have a say in this.
runners.comment: Below form on turf last time but has won on dirt this year; could have a say

runners.name: Super Hong Kong
runners.spotlight: Dual turf winner this season (1m3f/1m2f); back in good form last month, making the frame twice (1m1f/1m3f), and this surface also suits (although he rarely runs on it); can make his presence felt.
runners.comment: Made the frame on both turf starts last month and this surface also suits; respected

runners.name: Hailtothevictors
runners.spotlight: Just held on to open account here (1m, dirt) in April and made the frame on next two dirt starts (1m1f/1m); soundly beaten on turf since but still considered back on this surface.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten on turf last time but earlier dirt form gives him strong claims
runners.owner: Graham Kot Li Heng & Amy Chau

races.race_name: HKU Faculties Of Dentistry And Social Sciences Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)

runners.name: Better And Better
runners.spotlight: Only 1-14 but he's likeably consistent and emerged from midfield to go down narrowly over C&D three weeks ago; up 2lb.
runners.comment: Emerged from midfield to go down narrowly over C&D three weeks ago; up 2lb

runners.name: Zouper Fellow
runners.spotlight: Made virtually all over 5f here in April but went up 7lb and has struggled since.
runners.comment: Made virtually all over 5f here in April but went up 7lb and has struggled since
runners.owner: Dr Philip Ho Kin Hoi, Jason Ho Chung Man Et Al

runners.name: Power Patch
runners.spotlight: A down-the-field outsider on all four stats (6f-7f) and doesn't look up to this.
runners.comment: A down-the-field outsider on all four stats (6f-7f) and doesn't look up to this

runners.name: Master Payment
runners.spotlight: Twice runner-up here over 5f, last time under Purton from an unfavourable draw; has dropped luckier in that respect this time and must have every chance if seeing this out.
runners.comment: Twice runner-up here over 5f, last time under Purton from an unfavourable draw

runners.name: Yeux De Lifeline
runners.spotlight: Unplaced all six starts and there have been no obvious signs of progressing.
runners.comment: Unplaced all six starts and there have been no obvious signs of progressing

runners.name: E Hopeful
runners.spotlight: Has improved with each run, last time stepped up to 6f here and beaten 1.5l; didn't get the clearest of runs, either; stall 12 is the likely stumbling block.
runners.comment: Has improved with each run, last time stepped up to 6f here and beaten 1.5l

runners.name: Heroic Master
runners.spotlight: Reserve; exposed four-time winner but not in bad form as wasn't beaten too far when eighth at Happy Valley four days ago.
runners.comment: Reserve; exposed four-time winner but not in bad form; ran okay four days ago

runners.name: Next Fortune
runners.spotlight: Safely held in 6f/7f handicaps at this track and probably destined for a lower grade.
runners.comment: Safely held in 6f/7f handicaps at this track and probably destined for a lower grade

runners.name: Hyannis Star
runners.spotlight: Yet to get competitive in handicaps and opposable despite recent trial win.
runners.comment: Yet to get competitive in handicaps and opposable despite recent trial win

runners.name: Green Angel
runners.spotlight: Won a trial not too long ago but beaten in the region of 10l in his two official races, both here over 7f.
runners.comment: Won a trial not too long ago but beaten in the region of 10l in his two official races

runners.name: Trendy Rush
runners.spotlight: All three runs at 6f and no better than seventh in any of them; has dropped only 4lb.
runners.comment: All three runs at 6f and no better than seventh in any of them; has dropped only 4lb

runners.name: Sky Deep
runners.spotlight: Driven out for a breakthrough win over 7f here in March 2025 and off the track since; returns 5lb higher but twice ran second in trials last month, so fitness is no issue; respected, despite this drop back to 6f.
runners.comment: A winner in March 2025; recent trial runs confirm wellbeing; back at 6f but respected
runners.owner: Kwan Kin Man, Dora Kwan Man Ting & Bryan Kwan Hon Ming

runners.name: Vigor Fly
runners.spotlight: Figures of 625 in recent trials, ridden by Zac Purton in the last two; that rider jumps ship and this 3yo looks best watched on official debut.
runners.comment: Figures of 625 in recent trials, ridden by Zac Purton in the last two; he jumps ship

runners.name: Absolute Lucky
runners.spotlight: Reserve; failed to offer much in five runs for previous yard and remains best watched.
runners.comment: Reserve; failed to offer much in five runs for previous yard and remains best watched

races.race_name: HKU Faculties Of Law And Medicine Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)

runners.name: Stormy Knight
runners.spotlight: Unraced over this far but has finished no closer than sixth in his six races.
runners.comment: Unraced over this far but has finished no closer than sixth in his six races
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs James Tien Pei Chun

runners.name: Gor Gor
runners.spotlight: Has gone the wrong way since securing 6f honours here in February; 3lb above that winning mark.
runners.comment: Has gone the wrong way since securing 6f honours here in February

runners.name: Honorary
runners.spotlight: Yet to get within striking distance of the principals in seven races; mark is dropping if nothing else.
runners.comment: Yet to get within striking distance of the principals in seven races
runners.owner: Yip Yuk Kit & Dr Tommy Yip Chun Ming

runners.name: Solid Car
runners.spotlight: Has yet to win in Hong Kong; gives his running more often than not but hasn't been overly threatening despite his string of placed efforts; however, still one of the more appealing options in this field.
runners.comment: Not overly threatening despite getting placed but one of the more appealing options

runners.name: Master Lucky
runners.spotlight: More consistent than progressive, though it was his first run over 7f when beaten nearly 5l into third last time; more needed.
runners.comment: It was his first run over 7f when beaten nearly 5l into third last time; each-way claims

runners.name: Strathpeffer
runners.spotlight: Has plenty of form over C&D, including two competitive runs in last four races; beaten a length into fourth last time after producing some good late work from a tricky draw; definite chance.
runners.comment: Has plenty of form over C&D, including two competitive runs in last four races

runners.name: Polar Patch
runners.spotlight: Compromised by high draws in his first two official races (6f/7f) and ran better from stall 4 when close up over 6f here two weeks ago; however, now he's out in 11.
runners.comment: Lightly raced; good run from stall 4 here recently but now he's out in 11

runners.name: Star Satyr
runners.spotlight: On a good mark now and emerged from a dip in form when fourth over C&D this month; could go well, though consistency is not his forte and he's drawn wide.
runners.comment: Emerged from a dip in form when fourth over C&D this month

runners.name: Majestic Life
runners.spotlight: All races at 6f; better drawn now than last time but doesn't appeal as a winner waiting to happen.
runners.comment: All races at 6f; better drawn now than last time but doesn't look a winner in waiting

runners.name: Lucky Gibs
runners.spotlight: No real sign of winning potential at this level, on the track and in trials.
runners.comment: No real sign of winning potential at this level, on the track and in trials
runners.owner: Gibson Lau Wai Chi, Eric Lau Pui Kit & Ricky Lau Pui Wai

runners.name: Daily Trophy
runners.spotlight: Dirt is more his surface and he's now 0-17 on grass after latest heavy defeat.
runners.comment: Dirt is more his surface and he's now 0-17 on grass after latest heavy defeat
runners.owner: Peter Cheng & Jerry Cheng Chun Ho

runners.name: Chill Partners
runners.spotlight: C&D winner in March when 6lb lower; poorly drawn since but could still have run better than he did; again he's got a high stall.
runners.comment: C&D winner in March and poorly drawn since; again he's got a high stall

runners.name: Prestige Come
runners.spotlight: Hasn't been pulling up any trees in this year's trials and one to watch on official debut.
runners.comment: Hasn't been pulling up any trees in this year's trials and one to watch on official debut

runners.name: Splendor
runners.spotlight: Mostly well held in his trials and probably up against it off this initial mark.
runners.comment: Mostly well held in his trials and probably up against it off this initial mark

runners.name: Sunday's Serenade
runners.spotlight: Reserve; dual 1m Happy Valley winner in spring 2025 and was a close third over C&D in January; beaten in trials since but could be a factor if getting in.
runners.comment: Reserve; close third here in January; might be fitter for recent runs in trials

races.race_name: University Hall Handicap (Class 4) (Dirt)

runners.name: Allcash
runners.spotlight: Sole win was on turf in November; 0-10 on dirt but dropped into a Class 4 he did make the frame a month ago when behinid Night Purosangue and Chill Kaka; did well after starting slowly, though that is a habit of his.
runners.comment: 0-10 on dirt but dropped into a Class 4 he did make the frame a month ago

runners.name: California Star
runners.spotlight: Has won on the dirt here over 6f and 1m; however, his only one bright spot since April came in winning a trial.
runners.comment: Dual dirt winner here; his only one bright spot since April came in winning a trial

runners.name: Ariel
runners.spotlight: Can front run but accepted a lead when a rallying third to Yoda's Choice here recently; revised terms give him every chance of reversing those placings, especially with Nichola Yuen switching mounts; has since won a trial.
runners.comment: Third to Yoda's Choice here recently but on these terms he can gain his revenge

runners.name: Northern Beast
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner over 1m/1m1f here on turf and nearly went in again 18 days ago; hasn't had many chances on dirt (0-3); drawn in 13 but might have a say.
runners.comment: Nearly won for a fifth time on turf 18 days ago and not had many chances on the dirt
runners.owner: Ngai Man, Amy Wang Hong & Doris Ngai Yan Tung

runners.name: Leaping Star
runners.spotlight: 2-8 when trained in Ireland; mostly well held in Hong Kong (turf/dirt) and was behind Northern Beast last time.
runners.comment: Mostly well held in Hong Kong (turf/dirt) and was behind Northern Beast last time
runners.owner: Maurice Lai Tai Hei, John Lo Et Al

runners.name: Charity Gain
runners.spotlight: Has become well handicapped but no positives to take from recent runs and this is his first time on dirt.
runners.comment: No positives to take from recent runs and this is his first time on dirt
runners.owner: Ng Wing Shuen & Ng Wing Yin

runners.name: Glorious St Pauls
runners.spotlight: Had a favourable trip when fourth to Yoda's Choice here but that was a step forward and he's lightly raced.
runners.comment: Had a favourable trip when fourth to Yoda's Choice here but that was a step forward

runners.name: Bright Inheritance
runners.spotlight: Down the field on turf here two weeks ago and was beaten 7l on sole previous dirt start; over a year since the last of his two turf wins.
runners.comment: Down the field on turf here two weeks ago and was beaten 7l on sole previous dirt start
runners.owner: 23/24 Dennis C H Yip Trainer Syndicate

runners.name: Night Purosangue
runners.spotlight: From a wider draw than today's he did well to beat some of these comfortably 32 days ago; respected despite going up 7lb.
runners.comment: From a wider draw than today's he did well to beat some of these comfortably 32 days ago

runners.name: Chill Kaka
runners.spotlight: Both wins here on dirt over 1m; his form had run cold but he went off hard here a month ago and stuck around to be second best to Night Purosangue; this longer trip is worrying.
runners.comment: Back to form when a front-running second to Night Purosangue here recently

runners.name: Yoda's Choice
runners.spotlight: 0-23 on turf but made it 3-12 on dirt when making all the running over C&D a month ago; he's up 6lb and without Nichola Yuen's helpful 10lb claim, so has plenty more on his plate.
runners.comment: Made all here a month ago but up 6lb and without Nichola Yuen's useful 10lb claim
runners.owner: Alan Lee Yu Sing & Exors Of The Estate Of The Late Lee Kwok

runners.name: Perfect Team
runners.spotlight: Dropped into a Class 4 he was a close fifth over 1m on dirt here two runs back; disappointingly failed to build on that when behind Yoda's Choice a month ago; if back on song he'd have a say.
runners.comment: Better effort two runs ago but then disappointed back here behind Yoda's Choice
runners.owner: Ringo Ng Yiu Kwok & Lisa Ng Chan Choi Wan

runners.name: Joltin
runners.spotlight: Promising 2yo in Ireland but has made no impact in Hong Kong; couldn't recommend.
runners.comment: Promising 2yo in Ireland but has made no impact in Hong Kong; couldn't recommend
runners.owner: Jack Chan Hoi, Brian Pong Chung Bon & Xin Yi

runners.name: Happy Universe
runners.spotlight: Edged out Yoda's Choice over C&D in April but well behind that same horse a month ago; wouldn't read too much into latest heavy defeat in a turf trial.
runners.comment: Edged out Yoda's Choice over C&D in April but well behind that same horse a month ago

races.race_name: HKU Heritage Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)

runners.name: Glorious Success
runners.spotlight: 1-9 in Australia and now had plenty of chances in Hong Kong; has threatened over C&D but looking hard to predict.
runners.comment: Has threatened over C&D but looking hard to predict

runners.name: Amazing Duck
runners.spotlight: On a losing run but had excuses two runs back and running well otherwise, last time finishing close up behind Bling Bling Genius and Lucky Year; enters the reckoning.
runners.comment: On a losing run but had excuses two runs back and running well otherwise

runners.name: Victor Supreme
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced; sent off favourite under Zac Purton over C&D last time and got behind before keeping on for a closing fifth behind Bling Bling Genius; one to consider.
runners.comment: Favourite here last time and got behind before keeping on for a closing fifth

runners.name: Reliable Dad
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 4yo who has shown enough in his last three races to believe this mark is manageable; he finished those on the front foot.
runners.comment: He has shown enough in his last three races to believe this mark is manageable
runners.owner: Chris So Chun Hung & Mandy Kwan

runners.name: Lucky Twin Stars
runners.spotlight: It's been a struggle in Class 3s and he never really counted in a Class 4 here two weeks ago; he has dipped beneath his only winning mark, though, and is well drawn.
runners.comment: Struggled in Class 3s and he never really counted in a Class 4 here two weeks ago

runners.name: Ping Hai Comet
runners.spotlight: Has slipped to a dangerous mark but ought to have been suited by return to 1m3f when down the field at Happy Valley in March.
runners.comment: Ought to have been suited by return to 1m3f when down the field at Happy Valley in March

runners.name: Lucky Year
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced and hadn't been getting much luck before finishing close up behind Bling Bling Genius, who made all; these revised terms give him every chance of gaining his revenge, provided stall 12 isn't a killer.
runners.comment: Hadn't been getting much luck before finishing close up behind Bling Bling Genius

runners.name: Ace
runners.spotlight: Made winning stable debut in Class 5 C&D contest in March, beating Bling Bling Genius; two good runs since, as he was nearest at the finish after losing his place three weeks ago; those runs were over 7f and this far could suit.
runners.comment: Has been running well over 7f for new yard and this far promises to suit

runners.name: Grand Turbo
runners.spotlight: Belied triple-digit odds when fourth over 1m1f (soft) but could never land a blow from off the pace three weeks ago (yielding); from stall 14, others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: One of his runs reads positively but, from stall 14, others have more pressing claims

runners.name: Bling Bling Genius
runners.spotlight: On a hat-trick and did well to make all from stall 12 over C&D a month ago, repelling Lucky Year; has another 6lb to deal with but something has clicked.
runners.comment: On a hat-trick and did well to make all from stall 12 over C&D a month ago; up 6lb

runners.name: Flying Boom
runners.spotlight: Beat Glorious Success here in February and then close up behind Mega Mastermind; chance on all that but has to rebound from two lesser performances.
runners.comment: Has the form to make an impact but has to rebound from two lesser performances
runners.owner: Raymond Tsang Heung Tak & Kenneth Tsang Heung Kan

runners.name: Ninja Derby
runners.spotlight: Beaten fair and square in his last two races at this level and seems to need Class 5s to get competitive.
runners.comment: Beaten fair and square in his last two races and seems to need Class 5s to get competitive
runners.owner: Wilson Chan Wai Kwok, Joseph Yim Wai Ho & Gary Ng Ka Wai

runners.name: Charming Legend
runners.spotlight: Reserve; regressive 5yo who appears to be going nowhere at the minute.
runners.comment: Reserve; regressive 5yo who appears to be going nowhere at the minute
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Boniface Ho Ka Kui

runners.name: Pearl Of Pang's
runners.spotlight: Regressive profile on the track and well behind in his last two trial races.
runners.comment: Regressive profile on the track and well behind in his last two trial races

runners.name: Mega Mastermind
runners.spotlight: 3yo who races prominently; his progress was halted when well held at Happy Valley but he's back here after finishing second in a trial and Purton takes over; drawn wide, though.
runners.comment: Backward step last time but his improvement had come here; Purton takes over

runners.name: Spicy Bonus
runners.spotlight: Reserve; his debut here over 5f wasn't devoid of promise but since beaten in a good few trials; big step up in distance.
runners.comment: Reserve; debut here over 5f wasn't devoid of promise but since beaten in a good few trials

races.race_name: The Hong Kong University Alumni Association Challenge Cup (Handicap) (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)

runners.name: Multisuperstar
runners.spotlight: Never dangerous over 7f on his two runs last month and has now been unplaced all eight starts.
runners.comment: Safely held over 7f on his two runs last month and has now been unplaced all eight starts

runners.name: Leading Dragon
runners.spotlight: Dropped out tamely here last month and is now 0-6 but was placed on consecutive C&D starts beforehand and has obvious each-way claims if judged on those efforts.
runners.comment: Disappointed last time but was placed twice over C&D in April/May; a possible

runners.name: Enjoy Golf
runners.spotlight: Belatedly doubled his Hong Kong tally when making all in Class 5 C&D contest last month; did that from a wide stall but has more on his plate now back up in grade.
runners.comment: Made all over C&D last month but has more on his plate in this higher grade

runners.name: Bullish Pride
runners.spotlight: Yet to show anything more than minor promise after seven starts and now eligible for a drop in grade.
runners.comment: Yet to show anything more than minor promise after seven starts
runners.owner: Wong Wing Keung & Cheung Ka Ho

runners.name: Flying Sniper
runners.spotlight: Unplaced all four starts but not beaten that far on any of those outings and Zac Purton (aboard for first two runs) is reenlisted; enters calculations, despite double-digit draw.
runners.comment: Not beaten far on any of four starts and Zac Purton is reenlisted today
runners.owner: Raymond Tsang Heung Tak & Kenneth Tsang Heung Kan

runners.name: Incredible Moment
runners.spotlight: Reserve; won readily from the front over C&D last month, his 14th start, but was hit with a 9lb rise for that and managed only fifth off new mark a fortnight later; needs to kick on again.
runners.comment: Hit hard by the handicapper for last month's C&D win; needs to kick on again

runners.name: Navas G
runners.spotlight: Placed over C&D on debut in April and was messed about at the start when only sixth here last month; drawn wide today but still has potential.
runners.comment: Placed on debut and had excuse when only sixth next time; still has potential

runners.name: Little Monster
runners.spotlight: Held on well to make all from stall 11 over C&D last month, opening his account at the tenth attempt; 6lb higher now but better drawn today and could plausibly kick on again.
runners.comment: Made all from stall 11 over C&D last month, and could plausibly kick on again

runners.name: Kol
runners.spotlight: Well down the field on both dirt starts; drawn on the inside for this turf debut but needs the surface switch to enable huge improvement.
runners.comment: Well down the field on both dirt starts; makes turf debut today

runners.name: Call Me Success
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo; suggested his turn might be near when placed over C&D in March but has a point to prove after lesser effort in April.
runners.comment: Four-race maiden; placed over C&D in March but needs to rebound from a lesser effort

runners.name: Virtus Glory
runners.spotlight: Bettered low-key debut when keeping-on fourth at Happy Valley last month; well drawn here and benefits from Nichola Yuen's very useful 10lb claim; high on the list.
runners.comment: Bettered low-key debut when fourth last month; well drawn; good 10lb claimer booked

runners.name: Big Big World
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to a 6f winner; appeared to have made some progress when fourth in his latest trial but is probably best watched on this official debut; tongue-tie/hood fitted.
runners.comment: Latest trial was more encouraging; best watched on this official debut, though

runners.name: Sight Dreamer
runners.spotlight: Reserve; gained this season's two wins over 1m but looked as good as ever when close second over C&D a fortnight ago; considered if he gets a run.
runners.comment: Dual 1m winner this season; as good as ever when close second over C&D two weeks ago

runners.name: Faithful Warrior
runners.spotlight: Out of a six-time sprint winner; has not shown enough in recent trials to warrant strong interest from stall 12 on this official debut.
runners.comment: Has not shown enough in trials to warrant strong interest on official debut
runners.owner: Cheuk Hon Kit, Roland Leung Wing Cheung Et Al

races.race_name: HKU Foundation Handicap (Class 3) (Course C) (Turf)

runners.name: Parents' Love
runners.spotlight: Bounced back to form with Class 4 C&D win in April and again ran well when fifth in this higher grade last month; not well drawn today, though, and others appeal more.
runners.comment: Back in good form lately but not well drawn today; others appeal more
runners.owner: Edmund Ho Kwok Shing, Joe Fong Fu Yuen Et Al

runners.name: Lightness Of Music
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 4yo who made winning debut over C&D for Chris So just under a year ago and returned from break with promising C&D fourth behind Straight To Glory for new stable last month; nicely drawn today; big player if that recent run brought him on.
runners.comment: Made promising stable debut over C&D last month and remains lightly raced; player

runners.name: Master Of All
runners.spotlight: Gained all five wins over 7f, the latest on seasonal debut in September; not disgraced over C&D when dropped back in trip last month but generally in poor form since December; look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Gained all five wins over 7f; generally in poor form since December; look elsewhere

runners.name: Sparkling Fellow
runners.spotlight: 0-14 in Hong Kong but gave good account when keeping-on third behind Straight To Glory over C&D last month and has now been placed four times over C&D this season.
runners.comment: 0-14 in Hong Kong but placed four times over C&D this season

runners.name: Dragon Four Seas
runners.spotlight: Left Manford Man after string of disappointing efforts this season and there has been no revival after two starts for Jamie Richards.
runners.comment: Seems badly to have lost his way this season
runners.owner: Dr Alan Lee Wai Kin, Dr Kelvin Chong Man Yuk Et Al

runners.name: Mickley
runners.spotlight: Runner-up twice over 6f this year but was unable to land a significant blow when dropped to 5f here last month and disappointed when back over 6f a fortnight later; others preferred.
runners.comment: Runner-up twice over 6f this year but not seen to same effect over C&D last month

runners.name: Metro Power
runners.spotlight: Made all over C&D on seasonal debut in September and added to his tally at Happy Valley (5f) in March; last month's C&D sixth was perfectly respectable but he might remain vulnerable off a mark this high.
runners.comment: Two 5f wins this season (one here); looks a shade vulnerable off current mark

runners.name: Lucky Candy
runners.spotlight: 0-7 in Hong Kong but placed on last three appearances; lost out only narrowly to Straight To Glory over C&D four weeks ago and is 5lb better off with him today; respected.
runners.comment: 0-7 in HK but placed on last three starts, the latest a close second over C&D in May
runners.owner: Yeung Wai Kwong & Lee Sheung Ping

runners.name: Run Run Sunrise
runners.spotlight: Scored over C&D in March, his third start, and was placed over C&D on next two outings; may still have more to offer and but disappointed here last month and moves up in grade today.
runners.comment: Had been in good form until disappointing C&D run last month; up in grade today
runners.owner: Tang Ming, James Tang Sau Yip Et Al

runners.name: Love Together
runners.spotlight: Kept on strongly to open Hong Kong account over 5f at Happy Valley in January and was placed off mark 3lb higher than today's on next outing; not in same form over 6f lately but is of renewed interest now dropped back in trip.
runners.comment: Not seen to best effect over 6f lately; of renewed interest now back over optimum trip

runners.name: Straight To Glory
runners.spotlight: Won twice over 6f at Happy Valley in January and coped well with drop back in trip when coming from of the pace for C&D success last month; up 6lb to career-high mark but still considered in current form.
runners.comment: Coped well with drop back in trip when winning from off the pace over C&D last month
runners.owner: Chan Tim Shing & Pang Wai Hak

runners.name: Ten Loves
runners.spotlight: Delivered on earlier promise when winning 6f maiden in Australia in September, his fourth start; drops back in trip for Hong Kong debut; market may guide.
runners.comment: 6f maiden winner in Australia in September; drops back in trip for Hong Kong debut

runners.name: Super Strong Kid
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who won twice over C&D in Class 4 last year and was subsequently placed twice over 6f here in this higher grade; absent since slightly disappointing C&D run in December but still enters calculations.
runners.comment: Absent since December but this low-mileage 4yo was a dual C&D winner last year
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Chan Hou Bo & Chan Jenny Chun Lai

runners.name: Invincible Steed
runners.spotlight: Ended four-race career in Australia with 5f maiden win last August; makes Hong Kong debut after 11-month layoff but at least he's nicely drawn towards the nearside rail.
runners.comment: Ended four-race career in Australia with 5f maiden win last August; Hong Kong debut

races.race_name: HKU Jockey Club Student Villages Handicap (Class 3) (Course C) (Turf)

runners.name: Spicy Standard
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who scored over C&D in September and went very close here in April; managed only a midfield finish when upped to this grade last month, though and has tough draw today.
runners.comment: Went close over C&D in April but managed only seventh in this higher grade last month

runners.name: Gustosisimo
runners.spotlight: Put two below-par runs behind him when creditable third over C&D from stall 13 last month; still well handicapped on early-season form and can be very dangerous from the plum inside berth today.
runners.comment: Creditable third from stall 13 over C&D last month; dangerous from inside berth today

runners.name: Lucky My Way
runners.spotlight: Out of form for Manfred Man in early part of this season and made low-key stable debut when midfield over C&D last month; not the most obvious answer.
runners.comment: Made low-key stable debut last month; not the most obvious answer
runners.owner: Patrick Wong Ho Yin, Ricky Choy Wing Kay Et Al

runners.name: Sugar Sugar
runners.spotlight: Reserve; scored in Class 4 at Happy Valley in December but recent efforts have been underwhelming and he needs only to drop another 1lb to be eligible for a return to Class 4.
runners.comment: Class 4 winner in December but recent effort have been underwhelming

runners.name: King Miles
runners.spotlight: Won twice at Happy Valley in early part of this season and is back on a good mark now but needs to up his game after some disappointing recent efforts.
runners.comment: Needs to up his game after some disappointing recent efforts
runners.owner: Bernie Yim Mei Fung & Yim Man Hay

runners.name: Thriving Brothers
runners.spotlight: Gained this season's win over 7f but has finished second over 6f on last two appearances, more recently when a neck behind Effortless Win here last month; likely to be in the shake-up again.
runners.comment: Runner-up on last two outings, losing out only by a neck here last month; respected
runners.owner: Yeung Chun Wang, Yeung Ho Wang & Yeung Chung Wang

runners.name: Celestial Hero
runners.spotlight: Consistent 4yo who was beaten under 1l when third over C&D last month; drawn much wider today but probably won't be far away.
runners.comment: Consistent 4yo; close third over C&D last month; considered, despite wide draw
runners.owner: Dr David Chain Chi Woo, Bernard Chain S Chiang & C C S Liang

runners.name: Chill Buddy
runners.spotlight: Opened Hong Kong account over C&D in February, his eighth attempt, and made the frame off new mark here in April; below form after slow start on latest outing but has another good draw today; a possible if back on song.
runners.comment: C&D winner in February; didn't fire last time but has possibilities from stall 2

runners.name: Lady's Choice
runners.spotlight: Went close over C&D on seasonal debut in October but steadily regressive since; will appreciate this ease in grade but has a double-digit draw; others have much more appealing profiles.
runners.comment: Went close over C&D on seasonal debut in October but steadily regressive since
runners.owner: HK Association Of Lady Horse Lovers Syndicate

runners.name: High Praise
runners.spotlight: Ex-Australian 4yo; struggled to get competitive on last month's Hong Kong debut (6f, dirt); needs this return to turf to show him in a much better light.
runners.comment: Ex-Australian 4yo; struggled to get competitive on last month's HK debut
runners.owner: Anthony Poon Tak Ching, Vivica Poon Ip Sze Man et al

runners.name: Effortless Win
runners.spotlight: Won on debut in Australia last July and maintained 100% record when holding on from Thriving Brothers to score by a neck over C&D on last month's Hong Kong debut; 7lb higher now but could still have a lot more to offer.
runners.comment: Scored over C&D on last month's Hong Kong debut and could have a lot more to offer
runners.owner: 25/26 Frankie Lor Fu Chuen Trainer Syndicate (2)

runners.name: Stay Cosmic
runners.spotlight: 1-6 in Australia; absent since down-the-field finish in 1m Group 1 in October and probably best watched on Hong Kong debut.
runners.comment: 1-6 in Australia; absent since October; best watched on Hong Kong debut

runners.name: Mount Everest
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner in Class 4 last year but found things tougher in this grade on his two starts in early 2026; returns from four-month break.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner in Class 4 last year; found things tough in this grade in early 2026

runners.name: Shanghai Warrior
runners.spotlight: Reserve; made winning debut in 5.5f maiden in Australia in January; will be making his Hong Kong debut if he gets a run today.
runners.comment: Won over 5.5f on debut in Australia in January; this will be his Hong Kong debut

races.race_name: The Hong Kong Jockey Club Scholarships Handicap (Class 2) (Course C) (Turf)

runners.name: Victor The Winner
runners.spotlight: C&D Group 1 winner in his prime; has plummeted down the weights this season but stopped the rot and caused 58-1 surprise at Happy Valley last month (made all); interesting to see whether his revival gathers momentum.
runners.comment: Arrested decline and caused 58-1 shock at Happy Valley last month

runners.name: Kaholo Angel
runners.spotlight: Dead-heated at Happy Valley when upped to 1m in December but subsequent 6f-1m form has been underwhelming and he's drawn wide today.
runners.comment: This year's form has been underwhelming and he's drawn wide today
runners.owner: Ho Garrick Sai Man & Tam Wing Yee

runners.name: Magic Control
runners.spotlight: Very well handicapped on early-season form but just a respectable fifth at Happy Valley (5f) on latest outing in April and evidently not firing on all cylinders.
runners.comment: Very well handicapped on early-season form but not firing on all cylinders lately

runners.name: Rising Force
runners.spotlight: Improving 4yo who has won twice over C&D this year and went close on his last two outings, more recently behind Victor The Winner at Happy Valley 18 days ago; 5lb better off with him here and has good draw; major player.
runners.comment: Progressive 4yo; two C&D wins this year; went close on last two outings; well drawn
runners.owner: Tai Woon Shing & Chan Koon Man

runners.name: Public Attention
