runners.spotlight: Signed off for David Hayes with two down-the-field runs but got back on track with pretty good C&D third for new stable last month; well drawn again today and still on a workable mark; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Placed on recent stable debut; remains on workable mark and has another good draw

runners.name: Glowing Praises
runners.spotlight: Scored over 5f here on Hong Kong debut in February and has posted some good efforts in defeat over 5f/6f since; below form behind Victor The Winner at Happy Valley recently but still considered here.
runners.comment: Below form at Happy Valley recently but has generally made bright start to HK career

runners.name: Hot Delight
runners.spotlight: Very promising 3yo who launched career with three C&D wins at start of this year; had to settle for fourth when upped to this grade last month and has another double-digit draw today but remains of strong interest.
runners.comment: Began career with three C&D wins this year; only fourth last time but remains of interest

runners.name: Beauty Bolt
runners.spotlight: Campaign mainly over 1m this season (won over that trip in November); dropped back to 7f with respectable sixth last month but has been deserted by Zac Purton before this first ever run in a sprint.
runners.comment: Ran okay when dropped back to 7f last month; this is his first run in a sprint
runners.owner: Eleanor Kwok Law Kwai Chun & Patrick Kwok Ho Chuen

runners.name: Pakistan Legacy
runners.spotlight: Made all over C&D in March and ran well behind Hot Delight three weeks later but has a point to prove after duff effort in April.
runners.comment: Made all over C&D in March but needs to bounce back from a duff effort in April

runners.name: We Are Hero
runners.spotlight: Gained all six Hong Kong wins over 5f here, the latest a dead-heat in March; well beaten at triple-digit odds over 7f twice since and looks opposable again, even though drop back to 6f ought to help.
runners.comment: Gained all six HK wins over 5f; twice well beaten over 7f lately; also opposable over 6f

runners.name: Storm Rider
runners.spotlight: Three wins this season, the most notable a last-gasp success in 7f Group 3 handicap in January; well beaten when dropped back to 6f at Happy Valley last month but has much better draw here and good 10lb claims Nichola Yuen is booked.
runners.comment: Well beaten when dropped back to 6f last month but has much better draw this time

runners.name: Young Champion
runners.spotlight: Generally in good form this year but has a tough draw today and needs to rebound from a disappointing effort four weeks ago.
runners.comment: Generally in good form this year but not last time and has tough draw today

races.race_name: HKU Jockey Club Tower Handicap (Class 3) (Course C) (Turf)

runners.name: Chill Easy
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish 4yo who has been placed over C&D on the last two of his five Hong Kong starts; well drawn today; seems likely to be on the premises again.
runners.comment: Placed over C&D on last two of his five HK starts; probably in the mix again

runners.name: Kempes
runners.spotlight: Suffered dip in form after C&D win in early part of this season but returned from three-month break with quite encouraging C&D fourth in May; much better drawn today and back on a workable mark; could have a say.
runners.comment: Returned from break with encouraging C&D fourth in May; much better drawn today
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Colin Lo Chor Cheong

runners.name: Kansas
runners.spotlight: Reserve; smart when based in Ireland but well down the field all five Hong Kong starts this season (5f-7f).
runners.comment: Smart in Ireland but well down the field all five Hong Kong starts

runners.name: King Equine
runners.spotlight: Won twice in Australia last year (7f/6.5f) but was towards rear throughout last month's Hong Kong debut (6f) and remains best watched.
runners.comment: Towards rear throughout recent Hong Kong debut; remains best watched

runners.name: Well Enough
runners.spotlight: Fairly useful handicapper when based in Britain but a down-the-field outsider over C&D on last month's Hong Kong debut.
runners.comment: A down-the-field outsider over C&D on last month's Hong Kong debut

runners.name: Rising From Ashes
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner in Hong Kong, over trips ranging from 6f to 1m1f; suffered worrying mid-season slump in form but, following a 12-week break, took big step back in right direction when running-on fifth over 6f here last month; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Took big step back in right direction when running-on fifth over 6f last month

runners.name: The Unique Star
runners.spotlight: Placed on two of his five Hong Kong starts, more recently when third over C&D (made the running) last month; still unexposed over this trip; no surprise if he gives this another good shot.
runners.comment: Third over C&D (led) last month and remains unexposed over 7f; can make bold bid

runners.name: M M Concord
runners.spotlight: Unplaced all four Hong Kong starts; ran quite well from the front when fifth over C&D in April but well beaten over 1m since; others have much stronger claims.
runners.comment: Unplaced all four Hong Kong starts; others have much stronger claims

runners.name: Somelovefromabove
runners.spotlight: Reserve; won consecutive 6f Class 4 races in February/March and has been placed in this higher grade since; still unexposed over 7f; will have each-way claims if he gets a run.
runners.comment: Dual 6f Class 4 winner this year and placed in this higher grade since
runners.owner: Ling Wai Sun & Michael Ling Siu Ming

runners.name: Chiu Chow Spirit
runners.spotlight: Now 9lb lower than when going close over C&D in September but has been badly out of form in recent months and needs to turn a corner.
runners.comment: Badly out of form in recent months and needs to turn a corner
runners.owner: Charlie Lam Leung Seng & Michael Lam Yau Keng

runners.name: Thunder Action
runners.spotlight: Won over today's trip in Australia last year and belied 45-1 odds when keeping-on fourth over 6f here on last month's Hong Kong debut; drawn wide today but still looks interesting on this return to 7f.
runners.comment: Won over 7f in Australia last year and made promising HK debut over 6f last month

runners.name: Run Run Smart
runners.spotlight: Dominated from the front under Nichola Yuen over C&D last month and has now won three times this season; evidently in prime form but combination of latest 9lb rise and a rise in grade asks quite a lot of him today.
runners.comment: Dominated from the front over C&D last month; 9lb higher in higher-grade race now

runners.name: Thunder Kit
runners.spotlight: Came good in Hong Kong at sixth attempt, with ready 6f course win last month; went up 6lb for that but could quite plausibly have untapped potential over this extra furlong; high on the list.
runners.comment: Ready 6f course winner last month, his sixth HK start; tries a new trip today
runners.owner: Ian Chan Yau Nam & Cyrus Chan Sing Kit

runners.name: Make You Smile
runners.spotlight: Group 3 winner as a 2yo in Britain; launched Hong Kong career with two low-key runs this season but last month's C&D fourth was a pleasing step forward and he's better drawn today.
runners.comment: Safely held on first two HK starts but last month's C&D fourth was much better
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Tony Tang Wai Bun And Vicky Tang

runners.name: Fit For Beauty
runners.spotlight: Progressive 4yo who has collected three course wins (7f-1m) this season and was placed over C&D on last three appearances; has double-digit draw for third consecutive time but ought to be in the thick of things again.
runners.comment: Three wins this season; placed over C&D on last three outings; big player again

runners.name: True Brothers
runners.spotlight: 2-8 when based in Australia, the latter win gained in 6.5f handicap in April 2025; absent since a lesser effort in November and entitled to benefit from this Hong Kong debut.
runners.comment: 2-8 in Australia; makes Hong Kong debut after seven-month absence
runners.owner: 25/26 Me Tsui Yu Sak Trainer Syndicate

races.race_name: Prix Melisande (Listed Race) (Fillies) (Turf)
Prix Paul de Moussac (Group 2) (Turf)

runners.name: Nighttime
runners.spotlight: Much the best of these as a 2yo, completing a hat-trick when landing the C&D Prix la Rochette on soft in September before a second in the Group 1 Prix Jean-Luc Lagardere, again here over 7f; looks to have trained on and the drop to 7f probably a positive; leading chance.
runners.comment: Lots of good form as a 2yo and looks to have trained on; leading chance

runners.name: Afandy
runners.spotlight: Winner of three of his four starts, only missing out by a short-neck in a Cagnes-Sur-Mer Listed race on very soft in February when beaten; produced a clear career best in landing the 7f Group 3 Prix Djebel from the front on good to soft when last seen in early April and obvious claims if anywhere close to that level after his break.
runners.comment: Produced a clear career best in landing a Group 3 from the front last time; obvious claims

runners.name: Canalejas
runners.spotlight: Proved to be fairly consistent in busy enough 2yo campaign but was almost 2l behind Go Man in a Listed event at Toulouse last November (1m, heavy); not at his best on his return but then won a C&D Listed race in mid-May on very soft, so heads here in form, with the only question to answer being whether he can back that up.
runners.comment: Gained a clear career best last time but needs to prove he can back that up

runners.name: Good Bye Manu
runners.spotlight: Won a race at Cholet last June but produced by far his best performance in the Group 1 Poule d'Essai Des Poulains here last time, only beaten 5.5l, ahead of Imperior but a place behind Nighttime; drop in trip may help but no doubt more is needed here.
runners.comment: Ran really well in the Poulains here last time, but no doubt more is needed here
runners.owner: Mme Geraldine Reille-Villedey, Butel & Beaunez Et Al

runners.name: Synaran
runners.spotlight: Smart sort who won over 7f as a juvenile but took his form to a new level following a gelding operation, winning on the AW at Chantilly in March over a mile, before landing a C&D Listed race on good to soft in late April; well worth another step up in class for leading connections.
runners.comment: Done well since a gelding operation and well worth another step up in class

runners.name: Imperior
runners.spotlight: Didn't improve on his debut win as a 2yo for rest of that campaign, but won fresh again as a 3yo; didn't run too badly last time in the Group 1 Poule d'Essai Des Poulains on the figures, albeit well beaten, so slight concern is he's best after a break; cheekpieces go on for first time.
runners.comment: Smart performer at his best but could need to be fresh; cheekpieces go on

runners.name: Go Man
runners.spotlight: Twice a winner as a 2yo, which included a mile Listed event on heavy ground at Toulouse, when Canalejas was third; comeback run was decent when a length behind Afandy (giving that rival 4lb) but then no showed at Meydan; latest start was better though, which was here over a mile on very soft, but a long way below this grade of race; others preferred.
runners.comment: Has plenty of nice pieces of form but bit more exposed than some he meets
runners.owner: Christian Faure, J-M Lefebvre et al

runners.name: Pitch Perfect
runners.spotlight: Unbeaten in last three starts, two since being gelded, showing improving form at this trip in doing so; those wins came in a pair of handicaps and a conditions race, though, so faces a completely different calibre of rival in Group company; can do better but clearly needs plenty of improvement.
runners.comment: Comes into this in good form but this is a big step up in class

runners.name: Cielo Di Roma
runners.spotlight: Won twice as a juvenile, which included a Listed race success on the AW at Deauville over 7f, but ran moderately in the Saudi Derby on dirt on his comeback; performed better back in France since, albeit he has almost 4l to find with Afandy from their clash in the Prix Djebel, so needs a step forward in form.
runners.comment: Talented sort but doesn't have the scope for improvement some have
runners.owner: Mike Pietrangelo, Mag Horse Racing Srl et al

races.race_name: KRA La Coupe (Group 3) (Turf)

runners.name: Familly
runners.spotlight: Won on debut last March but didn't add to that until winning on her 4yo return just over three weeks ago in a 1m2f Listed event at Le Lion-D'Angers on soft; this will be tougher up against male opponents but should give a good account.
runners.comment: Won a 1m2f Listed event at Le Lion-D'Angers on soft recently; back against males today

runners.name: Tajlina
runners.spotlight: Made a promising start to her career but had a long break after coming up short in Listed company as a 2yo; mostly consistent since despite another absence, which includes a Group 3 win on very soft at Saint-Cloud, and chased home a decent sort at that same venue last time, recording a career best; appears to have limitations but should be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Classy filly who comes into this off a career best; should be thereabouts

runners.name: Trust On
runners.spotlight: Won three times last year all at mile on soft or heavy ground, including a Listed race at Saint-Cloud; form a bit mixed this campaign but a repeat of the level he ran to in the Prix Edmond Blanc in March could see him involved; not raced over this trip since April 2025.
runners.comment: Good chance of being involved on Prix Edmond Blanc run but more exposed than some

runners.name: Nitoi
runners.spotlight: Good enough to land a 1m2f Listed race at Chantilly last year early on (really strong form) and added another contest at that level at the end of the season at the same venue; shaped with promise on his return recently here over a mile and should enjoy going back up in trip; retained jockey Maxime Guyon appears to prefer Sinileo, though.
runners.comment: Has some sound form and ran well on comeback but retained jockey Maxime Guyon on Sinileo

runners.name: Snow Player
runners.spotlight: Knows how to win on varying surfaces, including the snow at St Moritz, but this is a significantly harder challenge than when successful in a handicap at Munich at the start of May from a rival who was well beaten next time; plenty to find.
runners.comment: Knows how to win on varying surfaces but really hard task at this level

runners.name: Sinileo
runners.spotlight: Swiftly upped in grade after a debut win just over a year ago into the Group 1 Prix du Jockey Club, where he ran with great credit; unsurprisingly not good enough to beat Daryz or Bay City Roller next time in a 1m2f Group 2 but won on his comeback, following a lengthy break, at Bordeaux Le Bouscat; should have lots more to offer and makes appeal.
runners.comment: Lightly raced colt with plenty of solid form; should have lots more to offer

runners.name: Casapueblo
runners.spotlight: Experienced performer who comes into this chasing a hat-trick following wins on the AW at Chantilly and a Listed event here over a furlong shorter after tongue-tie was applied; not won a Group race yet but placed in some, and this looks a good chance to change that statistic; seems to handle most ground.
runners.comment: Not won a Group race yet but comes into this in good form; seems to handle most ground

races.race_name: Prix Volterra (Listed Race) (Filllies) (Turf)

runners.name: Land Of Liberty
runners.owner: Guy Pariente Hold & Tars Farm Equestrian
